# How the Hybrid Simulator Works A friendly tour for engineers new to this codebase. ## The Problem in One Paragraph A pure RTL simulator (`vsim`, built on Verilator + SystemC) is **cycle-accurate**: it tells you exactly how many cycles every instruction takes inside the CPU pipeline. The price is speed: it runs at ~5-9 KHz. Booting Linux, running libc init, and warming up before reaching the actual code you care about can take hours. Meanwhile, QEMU runs the same program **functionally** at MHz speed, but it has no notion of pipeline cycles. The hybrid simulator combines them: run the boring parts in QEMU, switch to vsim for the small region of interest (ROI) you actually want to measure, and (optionally) hand back to QEMU to finish. ## High-Level Concept Five big ideas make this possible: 1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant. 2. **All architectural state fits in one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ...), PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr), V scalar CSRs (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vlenb), and the full V regfile (`v0..v31`, up to 2 KB) all live in `struct hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`). That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on. 3. **vsim only refreshes what the ROI can mutate.** vsim restores into the hart only what it needs to run (GPRs + PC + V state); on the way out it drains back only what could have changed (GPRs + PC + V state). Everything else (FP, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regfile bytes when the ROI doesn't touch them) **passes through unchanged** from the QEMU 1 snapshot to the QEMU 2 restore. This skips ~85+ DMI transactions per handoff. 4. **The trigger is a magic CSR write or a PC match.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` enters vsim; `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` exits it. CSR `0x7C0` is in the user-defined range. A third form, `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1`, decides at runtime based on `rs1`'s value (1 = exit). PC-trigger mode skips the magic CSR entirely - the orchestrator names entry/exit PCs on the CLI. 5. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording. ## State Categories and Their Transport The wire struct is uniform; transport per category depends on what QEMU's plugin API and the AX45MPV Debug Module are willing to expose. | Category | QEMU 1 -> snapshot | snapshot -> vsim hart | vsim hart -> snapshot | snapshot -> QEMU 2 | |---------------------------------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------| | GPRs `x1..x31`, PC | TCG plugin reads | DM Abstract Commands (DPC for PC) | DM Abstract Commands (fresh read) | gdbstub `G` packet | | `mtvec` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command | passthrough (not re-read) | gdbstub `P` | | Other M-mode CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `satp`) | TCG plugin | passthrough (not written) | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `kCsrByName`) | | FPRs `f0..f31` | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `hybrid_fpr_names`) | | PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr) | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (each slot) | | V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl` | TCG plugin | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command, **with Program-Buffer fallback** if the DM rejects | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB) | TCG plugin | shared-mmap scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`); fixture executes `vle8.v` to load | fixture executes `vse8.v` back to scratch; vsim memcpys those bytes into `state.vreg[]` | gdbstub `P` (per V-reg index) | | `vlenb` | TCG plugin | not restored (read-only, fixed by NDS_VLEN) | passthrough (verified, not re-read) | not sent | | Guest memory | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | "Passthrough" means: vsim copies the field from the inbound snapshot into the outbound snapshot unchanged. The hart never sees it; the field rides from QEMU 1 directly to QEMU 2. ## Trigger Modes Two ways to delimit the ROI; pick at runtime via `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`. | Mode | Entry into vsim | Exit out of vsim | Source of truth | |------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------| | CSR | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==0`) | `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==1`) | the binary itself | | PC | plugin arg `enter_pc=0x...` (no insn match) | vsim CLI `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` (synthetic hit added via `CsrTrigger::add_pc_hit`) | the orchestrator/CLI; binary is unchanged | Three handoff kinds (`hybrid_handoff_kind` in `include/hybrid/insn_match.h`): - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER` - QEMU drains and exits; vsim ignores at retire. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT` - vsim drains and exits; QEMU does not encounter this PC. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` - vsim decodes `rs1` from the matched insn, reads its current GPR value, fires only when it equals 1. Lets a single binary parameterize entry vs exit at runtime. ## Design Rationale **One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth (vxsat/vxrm/vcsr were added by consuming `reserved` rather than bumping to v2). **QEMU 1 -> vsim is file-based, vsim -> QEMU 2 is gdbstub-based.** QEMU mainline is used **as-is** (no patches, no fork). It exposes two relevant extension points: the TCG plugin API can *read* state during execution, and gdbstub `-S` can *write* state before the first fetch. Plugins cannot inject state at startup, and there is no `--load-state-from-file` flag. Each direction picks the only working primitive: the plugin drains to a file out of QEMU 1, and gdbstub `G`/`P` packets seed QEMU 2. The vsim side translates between the file and the RSP packets - QEMU never has to know about `hybrid_state_v1`. **Passthrough is the largest performance lever.** A naive design would round-trip every field of `hybrid_state_v1` through DM Abstract Commands - hundreds of DMI transactions. Instead, `HandoffController::set_passthrough_input(snapshot)` keeps QEMU 1's drained state in vsim memory; when the exit trigger fires, `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only x1..x31 + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs via DM, and merges those over the snapshot. Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight. **The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage. **Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore. **The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory. **Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level. ## Architecture Flowchart ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph DISK["Shared backing files"] SHM[("/dev/shm RAM
(mmap shared)
+ V scratch
at 0x80FF0000")] STATE_Q["state_qemu.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] STATE_V["state_vsim.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] end subgraph QEMU1["QEMU 1 (functional, MHz)"] Q1_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q1_PLUGIN["hybrid_handoff.so
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,0
PC mode: enter_pc=0x..."] Q1_TCG --> Q1_PLUGIN end subgraph VSIM["vsim (cycle-accurate, KHz)"] V_PIPE["RTL pipeline
(Verilator + SystemC)"] V_DM["JTAG DM driver (in-process)
Abstract Cmds + Program Buffer"] V_TRIG["CsrTrigger
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,1 (or runtime form)
PC mode: --hybrid-exit-pc"] V_CTRL["HandoffController
passthrough(snapshot) +
fresh GPR/PC/V-CSR drain"] V_DM -.GPR/FPR/CSR + vsetvl.-> V_PIPE V_PIPE -.retire signals.-> V_TRIG V_TRIG --> V_CTRL V_CTRL --> V_DM end subgraph QEMU2["QEMU 2 (functional, MHz)"] Q2_GDB["gdbstub -gdb tcp::PORT -S"] Q2_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q2_GDB -.G + per-name P packets.-> Q2_TCG end Q1_PLUGIN -- "drain ALL state" --> STATE_Q Q1_PLUGIN -- "qemu_plugin_request_exit" --> VSIM STATE_Q -- "--resume-from-qemu (passthrough seed)" --> V_CTRL STATE_Q -- "ResumeDriver: GPR/PC/mtvec/V" --> V_DM V_DM -.stage V regs.-> SHM SHM -.drain V regs.-> V_DM V_CTRL -- "merge fresh + passthrough" --> STATE_V V_TRIG -- "exit code 200" --> QEMU2 STATE_V -- "RSP G + P packets" --> Q2_GDB Q1_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM V_PIPE <-->|"every load/store"| SHM Q2_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM ``` ## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file + DM Abstract Commands), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip. Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap: - `SEED` at `0x80100000` - 16 int32 elements, the input pattern. - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` - 2 KB region, the V regfile transport channel. ### What each phase does ``` Phase 1 (QEMU 1) Phase 2 (vsim) Phase 3 (QEMU 2) ----------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- seed[0..15] = pattern vle32.v v8..v15, (seed) vse32.v v8..v15, (out) csrwi 0x7C0, 0 vadd.vi v9, v9, +100 verify out matches vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH) seed (with v9 +100) csrwi 0x7C0, 1 SEMIHOST_EXIT 0 ``` ### State that crosses each handoff | Boundary | Memory | Fresh-read in vsim | Passthrough through vsim | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) | |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | QEMU 1 -> vsim | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly | (nothing yet) | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH | | in vsim | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH | -- | -- | -- | | vsim -> QEMU 2 | `SEED` unchanged | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM) | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg | ### What each step proves | Step | What it tests | |----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping). | | `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). | | Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. | | Final exit code 0 | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2. | A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic. ## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists. ### 1. Phase 2 (vsim) must be syscall-free; phase 1 and 3 (QEMU) get standard newlib C fixtures link against the Andes toolchain's virtual-hosting libgloss (`-lgloss_vh`), whose `_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/... all bottom out in the RISC-V semihosting magic triple (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Both QEMU launches enable semihosting (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`), so the triple is intercepted before reaching `mtvec`. vsim does NOT implement semihosting -- inside the ROI the triple routes to `mtvec` -> `m_trap_entry` -> trap loop. | Phase | Standard newlib I/O (`printf`, `fopen`, ...) | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak | What you can do | |---------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | QEMU 1 | works (via `-lgloss_vh` -> semihosting) | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) | full newlib stdio: `printf`, `puts`, `fwrite`, etc. | | vsim | TRAPS (newlib stdio -> `_write` -> semihosting ebreak -> `mtvec`) | not implemented -> trap loop | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no syscalls, no `_exit`. | | QEMU 2 | works (same `-lgloss_vh` path) | enabled | full newlib stdio. | The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) marks the boundary: ```c // ===== QEMU 1 window ===== // printf etc. are fine enter_vsim(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 0 -> hand off to vsim // ===== vsim window ===== // syscall-free: registers + memory only exit_vsim(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 1 -> hand off to QEMU 2 // ===== QEMU 2 window ===== // printf etc. are fine again ``` The forbidden-ness in phase 2 is transitive: anything that ultimately calls `_write`/`_exit`/`_open`/... (i.e. anything that emits via the libgloss syscall stubs) is out. Pure CPU functions are fine: `vsnprintf` into a buffer, `memcpy`, ``, inline asm including V instructions, `setjmp`/`longjmp`. The pattern for cross-phase output is "compute in phase 2, emit in phase 1 or 3." There is no third channel; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.** ### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely: - Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost. - Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost. - Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost. - Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins). If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C. ### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates `v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes. `rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example. ### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop. ### 5. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi` The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it. ### 6. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts: - `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`). - `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`). - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code). - Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`. Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim. ### 7. Microarchitectural state does not transfer Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them. ### Where each rule lives in the runtime | Concern | File | |-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address) | `runtime/rt_c.ld` | | `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear | `runtime/crt0.S` | | M-mode trap save/restore + fatal trap reporter | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` | | Newlib syscall stubs (`_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/...) via semihosting | `-lgloss_vh` from the Andes toolchain | | Phase markers (`enter_vsim`, `exit_vsim`) | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` | | `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) | | QEMU launch flags (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `tests/hybrid/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_shared_mem}.sh` (QEMU 1), `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (QEMU 2) | | C-fixture build line (`-nodefaultlibs -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc`) | `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` | For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-7 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`. ## Known Limitations These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list. ### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped **What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind. **Affected registers:** - All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`). - 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped. - All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`). - `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target). **Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP. **Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase. **Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block. ### L2. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording. ### L3. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug. ### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. vsim's only deterministic peripheral is shared memory (the `mmap` backing file); there is no console, no clock, no semihosting. Stick to register-and-memory work inside the ROI. ### L5. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it. ### L6. Single hart only (today) The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end. ## Where to Look Next - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract. - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{rt_c.ld, crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, rt_c_helpers.h}` - the shared C runtime: linker script, entry, trap reporter, phase-aware drain markers. Newlib syscall stubs come from the toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting), not from a project-local libgloss. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - per-fixture build rules; documents which `-march`/linker-script combination each tier uses. - `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest. ## Mental Model in One Sentence > Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
3	# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
4	# artifacts in this workspace.
5	#
6	# Env overrides:
7	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
8	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
9	#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
10	#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
11	#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
12	#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
13	#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
14	#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
15	#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
16	#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
17	#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
18	#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
19	#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
20	#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
25	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
26	HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
27	TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"
28	
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
32	  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
33	fi
34	
35	step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
36	ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
37	warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
38	fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
39	
40	require() {
41	  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
42	    fail "$1 not found: $2"
43	    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
44	    exit 1
45	  fi
46	}
47	require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
48	require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
49	require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"
50	
51	export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN
52	
53	REQUIRED_ELFS=(
54	  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
55	  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
56	  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
57	  rt_c_v_matmul
58	  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
59	  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
60	  icount_spin
61	)
62	need_build=0
63	for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
64	  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
65	done
66	if (( need_build )); then
67	  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
68	  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
69	fi
70	
71	# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
72	# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
73	# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
74	RT_TESTS=(
75	  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
76	  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
77	  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
78	  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
79	  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
80	  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
81	  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
82	  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
83	  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
84	  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
85	  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
86	  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
87	  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
88	  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
89	  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
90	  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
91	  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
92	)
93	
94	NEG_TESTS=(
95	  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
96	  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
97	  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
98	  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
99	)
100	
101	# Switching-mode demos (T1-T7 in TEST_ROADMAP.md). Each demo exercises
102	# one of the M1-M8 trigger pathways end-to-end. Run once (no
103	# csr/pc/both loop) because each script hard-codes its trigger choice.
104	DEMO_TESTS=(
105	  "demo_csr_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
106	  "demo_pc_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
107	  "demo_icount_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
108	  "demo_bbv_profile|bash $HARNESS/demo_bbv_profile.sh"
109	  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
110	  "demo_archive_replay|bash $HARNESS/demo_archive_replay.sh"
111	  "demo_simpoint_loop|bash $HARNESS/demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
112	)
113	
114	MODE="${MODE:-both}"
115	case "$MODE" in
116	  csr|pc|both) ;;
117	  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
118	esac
119	
120	default_jobs() {
121	  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
122	  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
123	  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
124	    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
125	    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
126	      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
127	      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
128	    fi
129	  fi
130	  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
131	  printf '%d\n' "$n"
132	}
133	JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
134	[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
135	  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }
136	
137	FILTER="${FILTER:-}"
138	
139	LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
140	mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
141	RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
142	LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
143	: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
144	: > "$LOCK_FILE"
145	
146	# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
147	# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
148	# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
149	# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
150	# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
151	# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
152	# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
153	run_one() {
154	  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
155	  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
156	  local start end elapsed status rc
157	  start=$(date +%s)
158	  set +e
159	  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
160	  rc=$?
161	  set -e
162	  end=$(date +%s)
163	  elapsed=$((end - start))
164	  case $rc in
165	    0)  status=PASS ;;
166	    77) status=SKIP ;;
167	    *)  status=FAIL ;;
168	  esac
169	  (
170	    flock 9
171	    case "$status" in
172	      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
173	      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
174	      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
175	    esac
176	    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
177	      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
178	  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
179	}
180	
181	# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
182	# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
183	# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
184	declare -a WORKPLAN=()
185	for tm in csr pc; do
186	  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
187	  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
188	    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
189	    rest="${entry#*|}"
190	    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
191	    harness="${rest##*|}"
192	    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
193	    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
194	    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
195	  done
196	done
197	for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
198	  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
199	  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
200	  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
201	  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
202	done
203	for entry in "${DEMO_TESTS[@]}"; do
204	  demo_name="${entry%%|*}"
205	  demo_cmd="${entry#*|}"
206	  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$demo_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
207	  WORKPLAN+=("demo|$demo_name|$demo_cmd")
208	done
209	
210	TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
211	step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"
212	
213	# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
214	# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
215	# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
216	# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
217	# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
218	# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
219	SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
220	mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
221	exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
222	rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
223	for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
224	  printf '.\n' >&8
225	done
226	
227	i=0
228	for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
229	  i=$((i+1))
230	  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
231	  w_rest="${w#*|}"
232	  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
233	  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
234	  read -r -u 8 _tok
235	  (
236	    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
237	    printf '.\n' >&8
238	  ) &
239	done
240	wait
241	exec 8>&-
242	
243	declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
244	declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
245	declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
246	declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
247	while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
248	  case "$r_status" in
249	    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
250	    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
251	    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
252	          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
253	  esac
254	done < "$RESULTS_FILE"
255	
256	# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
257	# deterministic post-run failure dump.
258	for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
259	  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
260	  w_rest="${w#*|}"
261	  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
262	  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
263	                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
264	  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
265	    echo
266	    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
267	    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
268	  fi
269	done
270	
271	echo
272	csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
273	pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
274	neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
275	dem_p=${MODE_PASS[demo]} dem_f=${MODE_FAIL[demo]} dem_s=${MODE_SKIP[demo]}
276	total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p + dem_p))
277	total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f + dem_f))
278	total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s + dem_s))
279	step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  demo ${dem_p}p/${dem_f}f/${dem_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
280	if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
281	  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
282	  exit 1
283	fi
284

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
  # icount_spin: CSR-less spin loop. Used by switching-mode demos
  # T3/T4/T5/T7 (icount, BBV emit, slice-consume, SimPoint loop).
  icount_spin
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

# Switching-mode demos (T1-T7 in TEST_ROADMAP.md). Each demo exercises
# one of the M1-M8 trigger pathways end-to-end. Run once (no
# csr/pc/both loop) because each script hard-codes its trigger choice.
DEMO_TESTS=(
  "demo_csr_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
  "demo_pc_oneshot|bash $HARNESS/demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
  "demo_icount_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
  "demo_bbv_profile|bash $HARNESS/demo_bbv_profile.sh"
  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip|bash $HARNESS/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
  "demo_archive_replay|bash $HARNESS/demo_archive_replay.sh"
  "demo_simpoint_loop|bash $HARNESS/demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$(mktemp -d -t e2e-XXXXXX)}"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
RESULTS_FILE="$LOG_DIR/results.tsv"
LOCK_FILE="$LOG_DIR/lock"
: > "$RESULTS_FILE"
: > "$LOCK_FILE"

# run_one MODE NAME CMD INDEX TOTAL
# Captures the test command's stdout+stderr into $LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log,
# emits one atomic progress line, and appends a TSV row to $RESULTS_FILE
# (mode<TAB>name<TAB>status<TAB>rc<TAB>elapsed). flock on $LOCK_FILE
# serializes the progress write and the results-file append so concurrent
# children (introduced by the parallel-execution commit) cannot interleave
# their TTY writes or corrupt the tally file.
run_one() {
  local mode="$1" name="$2" cmd="$3" idx="$4" total="$5"
  local log="$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log"
  local start end elapsed status rc
  start=$(date +%s)
  set +e
  bash -c "$cmd" >"$log" 2>&1
  rc=$?
  set -e
  end=$(date +%s)
  elapsed=$((end - start))
  case $rc in
    0)  status=PASS ;;
    77) status=SKIP ;;
    *)  status=FAIL ;;
  esac
  (
    flock 9
    case "$status" in
      PASS) ok   "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (${elapsed}s)" ;;
      SKIP) warn "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=77, ${elapsed}s)" ;;
      FAIL) fail "[$idx/$total] ${name}[${mode}] (rc=$rc, ${elapsed}s) -> $log" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\t%d\t%d\n' \
      "$mode" "$name" "$status" "$rc" "$elapsed" >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
  ) 9>"$LOCK_FILE"
}

# Build the workplan: one entry per (mode, name, cmd) tuple. Order
# matters: csr-mode tests first, then pc-mode, then negatives (matching
# the original sequential layout so logs are streamed in the same order).
declare -a WORKPLAN=()
for tm in csr pc; do
  [[ "$MODE" != "both" && "$MODE" != "$tm" ]] && continue
  for entry in "${RT_TESTS[@]}"; do
    rt_name="${entry%%|*}"
    rest="${entry#*|}"
    env_prefix="${rest%|*}"
    harness="${rest##*|}"
    [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$rt_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
    cmd="TRIGGER_MODE=$tm $env_prefix bash $harness"
    WORKPLAN+=("$tm|$rt_name|$cmd")
  done
done
for entry in "${NEG_TESTS[@]}"; do
  neg_name="${entry%%|*}"
  neg_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$neg_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("neg|$neg_name|$neg_cmd")
done
for entry in "${DEMO_TESTS[@]}"; do
  demo_name="${entry%%|*}"
  demo_cmd="${entry#*|}"
  [[ -n "$FILTER" && ! "$demo_name" =~ $FILTER ]] && continue
  WORKPLAN+=("demo|$demo_name|$demo_cmd")
done

TOTAL=${#WORKPLAN[@]}
step "logs: $LOG_DIR  jobs: $JOBS  mode: $MODE  tests: $TOTAL"

# FIFO-based bounded job pool. Each child reads one token from $SEM_FIFO
# before starting and writes one back on exit; only $JOBS tokens exist,
# so at most $JOBS children run concurrently. Opening the fifo in
# read-write mode (<>) means writes never block the parent. The fifo
# inode is unlinked immediately after fd 8 is bound -- the kernel keeps
# the pipe alive as long as fd 8 is open.
SEM_FIFO="$LOG_DIR/sem.fifo"
mkfifo "$SEM_FIFO"
exec 8<>"$SEM_FIFO"
rm -f "$SEM_FIFO"
for ((sem_i=0; sem_i<JOBS; sem_i++)); do
  printf '.\n' >&8
done

i=0
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_cmd="${w_rest#*|}"
  read -r -u 8 _tok
  (
    run_one "$w_mode" "$w_name" "$w_cmd" "$i" "$TOTAL"
    printf '.\n' >&8
  ) &
done
wait
exec 8>&-

declare -A MODE_PASS=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -A MODE_FAIL=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -A MODE_SKIP=([csr]=0 [pc]=0 [neg]=0 [demo]=0)
declare -a FAILED_NAMES=()
while IFS=$'\t' read -r r_mode r_name r_status _r_rc _r_el; do
  case "$r_status" in
    PASS) MODE_PASS[$r_mode]=$((MODE_PASS[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    SKIP) MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]=$((MODE_SKIP[$r_mode]+1)) ;;
    FAIL) MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]=$((MODE_FAIL[$r_mode]+1));
          FAILED_NAMES+=("${r_name}[${r_mode}]") ;;
  esac
done < "$RESULTS_FILE"

# Stream failed-test logs in workplan order so the user sees a
# deterministic post-run failure dump.
for w in "${WORKPLAN[@]}"; do
  w_mode="${w%%|*}"
  w_rest="${w#*|}"
  w_name="${w_rest%%|*}"
  w_status=$(awk -F'\t' -v m="$w_mode" -v n="$w_name" \
                 '$1==m&&$2==n{print $3; exit}' "$RESULTS_FILE")
  if [[ "$w_status" == "FAIL" ]]; then
    echo
    echo "=== FAIL log: ${w_name}[${w_mode}] ==="
    cat "$LOG_DIR/$w_mode--$w_name.log"
  fi
done

echo
csr_p=${MODE_PASS[csr]} csr_f=${MODE_FAIL[csr]} csr_s=${MODE_SKIP[csr]}
pc_p=${MODE_PASS[pc]}   pc_f=${MODE_FAIL[pc]}   pc_s=${MODE_SKIP[pc]}
neg_p=${MODE_PASS[neg]} neg_f=${MODE_FAIL[neg]} neg_s=${MODE_SKIP[neg]}
dem_p=${MODE_PASS[demo]} dem_f=${MODE_FAIL[demo]} dem_s=${MODE_SKIP[demo]}
total_pass=$((csr_p + pc_p + neg_p + dem_p))
total_fail=$((csr_f + pc_f + neg_f + dem_f))
total_skip=$((csr_s + pc_s + neg_s + dem_s))
step "summary: csr ${csr_p}p/${csr_f}f/${csr_s}s  pc ${pc_p}p/${pc_f}f/${pc_s}s  neg ${neg_p}p/${neg_f}f/${neg_s}s  demo ${dem_p}p/${dem_f}f/${dem_s}s  total ${total_pass} passed, ${total_fail} failed, ${total_skip} skipped"
if (( total_fail > 0 )); then
  fail "failures: ${FAILED_NAMES[*]}"
  exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T1: CSR insn-match switching mode (M1) + end-of-program (M8).
#
# Walks one round-trip with CSR triggers (csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1}) on a
# state-rich Tier-2 fixture (rt_c_v_regs). Demonstrates the default
# switching path a user gets out of the box.
#
# Honors all the same env overrides as rt_shared_mem.sh (VSIM_BIN,
# VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM, TIMEOUT).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)

MODE_LABEL="CSR insn match (M1+M8)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
echo "Trigger: csrwi 0x7C0, 0 (enter) -> csrwi 0x7C0, 1 (exit)"
echo "=========================================================="

set +e
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/rt_shared_mem.sh"
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
  exit 77
fi

if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
  exit "$RC"
fi

echo "[demo M1] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
3	#
4	# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
5	# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
6	# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
7	#
8	# Required environment:
9	#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
10	#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
11	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so
12	
13	set -euo pipefail
14	
15	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
16	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
17	
18	# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
19	# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
20	# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
21	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
22	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
23	TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
24	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
25	
26	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
27	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
28	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
29	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
30	NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"
31	
32	for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
33	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
34	    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
35	    exit 77
36	  fi
37	done
38	
39	case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
40	  csr) ;;
41	  pc)
42	    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
43	      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
44	      exit 77
45	    fi
46	    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
47	    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
48	    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
49	    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
50	               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
51	    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
52	              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
53	    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
54	      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
55	      exit 1
56	    fi
57	    ;;
58	  *)
59	    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
60	    exit 1
61	    ;;
62	esac
63	
64	# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
65	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
66	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
67	STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
68	STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
69	SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
70	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT
71	
72	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
73	
74	PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
75	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
76	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
77	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
78	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
79	fi
80	
81	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
82	# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
83	# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
84	# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
85	# under either VLEN.
86	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
87	# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
88	# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
89	# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
90	# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
91	# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
92	set +e
93	"$VSIM_QEMU" \
94	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
95	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
96	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
97	  -nographic \
98	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
99	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
100	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
101	QEMU1_RC=$?
102	set -e
103	
104	if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
105	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
106	  exit 1
107	fi
108	
109	if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
110	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
111	  exit 1
112	fi
113	
114	echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
115	set +e
116	timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
117	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
118	  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
119	  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
120	  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
121	  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
122	  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
123	  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
124	RC=$?
125	set -e
126	
127	if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
128	  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
129	  echo "--- sim log ---"
130	  cat "$SIM_LOG"
131	  exit 1
132	fi
133	
134	echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
135

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build hybrid_qemu out-of-tree under build/qemu/.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
BUILD="$ROOT/build/qemu"

if [[ ! -x "$SRC/configure" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/configure - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p "$BUILD"

if [[ ! -f "$BUILD/build.ninja" && ! -f "$BUILD/Makefile" ]]; then
  (cd "$BUILD" && "$SRC/configure" \
    --target-list=riscv64-softmmu \
    --enable-plugins \
    --disable-werror \
    --disable-libnfs \
    --disable-werror)
fi

make -C "$BUILD" CFLAGS="-Wno-int-conversion -Wno-error=redundant-decls" -j64

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
3	set -euo pipefail
4	
5	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
6	VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
7

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build vsim inside the env-stage container image.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"

if [[ ! -f "$VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $VSIM_DIR/Dockerfile - run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

cd "$VSIM_DIR"

podman build --target env -t vsim-env:latest .
podman run --rm \
  -v "$PWD:/work" \
  -v "$ROOT/include:/include:ro" \
  -w /work localhost/vsim-env:latest ./build.sh

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T2: PC symbol-resolve switching mode (M2).
3	#
4	# Same round-trip as T1 but the orchestrator resolves
5	# _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the ELF symbol table and
6	# threads enter_pc=0x... to the QEMU plugin and --hybrid-exit-pc=0x...
7	# to vsim. The csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1} insns in the binary become inert
8	# (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on
9	# andes-ax45mp). Demonstrates that the same binary can be driven
10	# without any in-source trigger markers.
11	
12	set -euo pipefail
13	
14	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
15	
16	MODE_LABEL="PC symbol resolve (M2)"
17	echo "=========================================================="
18	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
19	echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
20	echo "Trigger: nm-resolved _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"
21	echo "=========================================================="
22	
23	set +e
24	FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=pc EXPECT_RC=0 \
25	  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/rt_shared_mem.sh"
26	RC=$?
27	set -e
28	
29	if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
30	  echo "[demo M2] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
31	  exit 77
32	fi
33	
34	if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
35	  echo "[demo M2] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
36	  exit "$RC"
37	fi
38	
39	echo "[demo M2] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"
40

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T2: PC symbol-resolve switching mode (M2).
#
# Same round-trip as T1 but the orchestrator resolves
# _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the ELF symbol table and
# threads enter_pc=0x... to the QEMU plugin and --hybrid-exit-pc=0x...
# to vsim. The csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1} insns in the binary become inert
# (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on
# andes-ax45mp). Demonstrates that the same binary can be driven
# without any in-source trigger markers.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)

MODE_LABEL="PC symbol resolve (M2)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
echo "Trigger: nm-resolved _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"
echo "=========================================================="

set +e
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=pc EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/rt_shared_mem.sh"
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
  echo "[demo M2] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
  exit 77
fi

if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M2] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
  exit "$RC"
fi

echo "[demo M2] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end.
3	#
4	# Both QEMU1 and vsim switch via retired-instruction count alone, with
5	# no CSR markers in the workload:
6	#
7	#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1    -> drain at insn N1 (M3).
8	#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2     -> drain at insn N2 (M3).
9	#
10	# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less tight 2-insn RVC loop at 0x100002
11	# and 0x100004). Neither side could use csrwi 0x7C0 -- icount IS the
12	# only available drain trigger. There is no natural semihosting exit
13	# (icount_spin spins forever), so this demo asserts the round-trip
14	# DRAINS correctly at both ends; M8 is exercised by T1 / T2 / T6.
15	#
16	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
17	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
18	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
19	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
20	#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
21	#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 10000)
22	#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 5000)
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
27	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
28	
29	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
30	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
31	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
32	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
33	QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-10000}"
34	VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"
35	
36	MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3)"
37	echo "=========================================================="
38	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
39	echo "Fixture: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF") (CSR-less tight loop)"
40	echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
41	echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
42	echo "=========================================================="
43	
44	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
45	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo M3] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
46	done
47	
48	SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
49	SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
50	STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
51	STATE_V=$(mktemp)
52	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
53	VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
54	trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
55	truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
56	
57	read_u64() { python3 -c "
58	import struct
59	with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
60	    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
61	"; }
62	
63	echo "[demo M3 1/2] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
64	set +e
65	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
66	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
67	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
68	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
69	  -nographic \
70	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
71	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
72	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
73	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
74	QRC=$?
75	set -e
76	
77	if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
78	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
79	  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
80	fi
81	if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
82	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
83	fi
84	
85	PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
86	PC_Q_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_Q")
87	if [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
88	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 drained pc=$PC_Q_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
89	fi
90	echo "  QEMU1 drain: pc=$PC_Q_HEX"
91	
92	echo "[demo M3 2/2] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain"
93	set +e
94	timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
95	  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
96	  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
97	  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
98	  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
99	VRC=$?
100	set -e
101	
102	if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
103	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
104	  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
105	fi
106	if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
107	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim state empty"; exit 1
108	fi
109	
110	PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
111	PC_V_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_V")
112	if [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
113	  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
114	fi
115	echo "  vsim drain: pc=$PC_V_HEX"
116	
117	echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"
118

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end.
#
# Both QEMU1 and vsim switch via retired-instruction count alone, with
# no CSR markers in the workload:
#
#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1    -> drain at insn N1 (M3).
#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2     -> drain at insn N2 (M3).
#
# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less tight 2-insn RVC loop at 0x100002
# and 0x100004). Neither side could use csrwi 0x7C0 -- icount IS the
# only available drain trigger. There is no natural semihosting exit
# (icount_spin spins forever), so this demo asserts the round-trip
# DRAINS correctly at both ends; M8 is exercised by T1 / T2 / T6.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 10000)
#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 5000)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-10000}"
VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"

MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF") (CSR-less tight loop)"
echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo M3] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done

SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
STATE_V=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"

read_u64() { python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
"; }

echo "[demo M3 1/2] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
QRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
fi

PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
PC_Q_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_Q")
if [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 drained pc=$PC_Q_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
fi
echo "  QEMU1 drain: pc=$PC_Q_HEX"

echo "[demo M3 2/2] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim state empty"; exit 1
fi

PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
PC_V_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_V")
if [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
fi
echo "  vsim drain: pc=$PC_V_HEX"

echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T4: BBV-emit switching mode (M4) + SimPoint clustering integration.
3	#
4	# QEMU + plugin runs icount_spin.elf with bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M.
5	# Plugin writes one SimPoint .bb row per N retired insns; expected
6	# rows = M/N. Then tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint clusters the .bb file
7	# and produces simpts (slice -> cluster) + weights (cluster -> mass).
8	#
9	# Outputs are persisted under $WORKDIR (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
10	# so T5/T7 can consume them. WORKDIR is printed on PASS.
11	#
12	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
13	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
14	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
15	#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
16	#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary
17	#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for bbv/simpts/weights (default: mktemp -d)
18	#   SLICE         insns per BBV row (default: 1000)
19	#   ICOUNT        total insns before drain (default: 10000)
20	#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2; tiny BBVs need conservative K)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
25	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
26	TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)
27	
28	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
29	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T4: BBV-emit switching mode (M4) + SimPoint clustering integration.
#
# QEMU + plugin runs icount_spin.elf with bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M.
# Plugin writes one SimPoint .bb row per N retired insns; expected
# rows = M/N. Then tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint clusters the .bb file
# and produces simpts (slice -> cluster) + weights (cluster -> mass).
#
# Outputs are persisted under $WORKDIR (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
# so T5/T7 can consume them. WORKDIR is printed on PASS.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for bbv/simpts/weights (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row (default: 1000)
#   ICOUNT        total insns before drain (default: 10000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2; tiny BBVs need conservative K)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-bbv-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-1000}"
ICOUNT="${ICOUNT:-10000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"

MODE_LABEL="BBV emit + SimPoint cluster (M4)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: icount_spin (tight 2-insn loop, CSR-less)"
echo "Plugin: bbv_out=...,slice=$SLICE,icount=$ICOUNT"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "[demo M4] SKIP: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"
  exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV_OUT="$WORKDIR/icount_spin.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
STATE_OUT="$WORKDIR/state_qemu.bin"
QEMU_LOG="$WORKDIR/qemu.log"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-bbv-shared.XXXXXX)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

EXPECTED_ROWS=$((ICOUNT / SLICE))

echo "[demo M4 1/3] QEMU + plugin: emit BBV ($EXPECTED_ROWS rows expected)"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,icount=$ICOUNT,bbv_out=$BBV_OUT,slice=$SLICE" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200, got $RC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV_OUT")
if [ "$ROWS" -ne "$EXPECTED_ROWS" ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: expected $EXPECTED_ROWS BBV rows, got $ROWS"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo M4 2/3] SimPoint cluster: maxK=$MAXK"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV_OUT" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: simpoint exit $SP_RC"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ] || [ ! -s "$WEIGHTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: simpoint output empty (simpts=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS") weights=$(wc -c <"$WEIGHTS"))"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo M4 3/3] Chosen phases:"
echo "  simpts (slice_idx cluster_id):"
awk '{printf "    %s\n", $0}' "$SIMPTS"
echo "  weights (mass cluster_id):"
awk '{printf "    %s\n", $0}' "$WEIGHTS"

# Informational: weights should sum to ~1.0 (no hard assert; simpoint
# may emit weights in unnormalised form for some -maxK paths).
SUM=$(awk '{s+=$1} END{printf "%.4f", s}' "$WEIGHTS")
echo "  weight sum: $SUM"

echo "[demo M4] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): rows=$ROWS, clusters=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS"), workdir=$WORKDIR"
