1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T6: archive emit (M6) + checkpoint replay (M7).
3	#
4	# Step A (M6): orchestrator runs a single-spawn QEMU plan that drains
5	#   on the CSR-enter trigger and archives state.bin + mem + meta.json
6	#   under $WORKDIR/snap/0/.
7	# Step B (M7): orchestrator replay-checkpoint hydrates $WORKDIR/snap/0/
8	#   into a fresh /dev/shm mmap and resumes the chosen model (vsim by
9	#   default), proving the archive is portable across sessions.
10	#
11	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
12	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
13	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
14	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
15	#   VSIM_ELF      C-level Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
16	#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for snap + replay state (default: mktemp -d)
17	#   RESUME_WITH   vsim | qemu (default: vsim)
18	
19	set -euo pipefail
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
22	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
23	TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)
24	
25	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
26	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
27	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
28	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
29	WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-archive-XXXXXX)}"
30	RESUME_WITH="${RESUME_WITH:-vsim}"
31	
32	MODE_LABEL="Archive emit (M6) + checkpoint replay (M7)"
33	echo "=========================================================="
34	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
35	echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs"
36	echo "Step A: orchestrator run plan -> snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}"
37	echo "Step B: orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with $RESUME_WITH"
38	echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
39	echo "=========================================================="
40	
41	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
42	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
43	    echo "[demo M6+M7] SKIP: $f not built"
44	    exit 77
45	  fi
46	done
47	if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
48	  echo "[demo M6+M7] SKIP: python3 not found"
49	  exit 77
50	fi
51	
52	mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
53	PLAN_YAML="$WORKDIR/plan_archive.yaml"
54	ARCHIVE_ROOT="$WORKDIR"
55	RUN_WORKDIR="$WORKDIR/run"
56	REPLAY_WORKDIR="$WORKDIR/replay"
57	RUN_LOG="$WORKDIR/run.log"
58	REPLAY_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay.log"
59	
60	# Single-step plan: QEMU drains on CSR-enter and archives.
61	cat >"$PLAN_YAML" <<EOF
62	session:
63	  mem_size: 134217728
64	  mmap_path: null
65	  elf: hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf
66	
67	steps:
68	  - model: qemu
69	    trigger: { kind: csr }
70	    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }
71	EOF
72	
73	echo "[demo M6+M7 1/4] orchestrator run: drain + archive"
74	mkdir -p "$RUN_WORKDIR"
75	set +e
76	PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
77	  python3 -m orchestrator.main run "$PLAN_YAML" \
78	    --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" \
79	    --workdir "$RUN_WORKDIR" \
80	    --archive-root "$ARCHIVE_ROOT" \
81	    >"$RUN_LOG" 2>&1
82	RUN_RC=$?
83	set -e
84	
85	if [ "$RUN_RC" -ne 200 ] && [ "$RUN_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
86	  echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: orchestrator run exited $RUN_RC"
87	  tail -30 "$RUN_LOG"
88	  exit 1
89	fi
90	
91	SNAP_DIR="$ARCHIVE_ROOT/snap/0"
92	for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
93	  if [ ! -s "$SNAP_DIR/$f" ]; then
94	    echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: missing or empty $SNAP_DIR/$f"
95	    ls -la "$SNAP_DIR" 2>&1 || true
96	    tail -30 "$RUN_LOG"
97	    exit 1
98	  fi
99	done
100	
101	echo "[demo M6+M7 2/4] validate meta.json schema"
102	META_OK=$(python3 - "$SNAP_DIR/meta.json" <<'PY'
103	import json, sys
104	meta = json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))
105	assert meta.get("schema") == 1, f"schema {meta.get('schema')!r}"
106	assert meta.get("abi_version") == 1, f"abi_version {meta.get('abi_version')!r}"
107	assert meta.get("abi_magic", "").lower() in ("0x42594841", "42594841"), \
108	    f"abi_magic {meta.get('abi_magic')!r}"
109	assert meta.get("mem_size") == 134217728, f"mem_size {meta.get('mem_size')!r}"
110	print("schema=1 abi_version=1 abi_magic=0x42594841 mem_size=128MB")
111	PY
112	)
113	echo "  $META_OK"
114	
115	echo "[demo M6+M7 3/4] orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with $RESUME_WITH"
116	mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"
117	set +e
118	PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
119	  python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP_DIR" \

123	    >"$REPLAY_LOG" 2>&1
124	REPLAY_RC=$?
125	set -e
126	
127	if [ "$REPLAY_RC" -ne 0 ] && [ "$REPLAY_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
128	  echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: replay-checkpoint exited $REPLAY_RC"
129	  tail -30 "$REPLAY_LOG"
130	  exit 1
131	fi
132	
133	echo "[demo M6+M7 4/4] verify replay advanced PC past archive"
134	REPLAY_STATE=""
135	for cand in "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"/*state*.bin "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"/*.bin; do
136	  if [ -s "$cand" ]; then
137	    REPLAY_STATE="$cand"
138	    break
139	  fi
140	done
141	if [ -z "$REPLAY_STATE" ]; then
142	  echo "  no post-replay state file produced (replay completed at exit; OK)"
143	else
144	  ARCHIVE_PC=$(python3 -c "
145	import struct
146	print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('$SNAP_DIR/state.bin', 'rb').read()[16:24])[0]))")
147	  REPLAY_PC=$(python3 -c "
148	import struct
149	print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('$REPLAY_STATE', 'rb').read()[16:24])[0]))")
150	  echo "  archive pc=$ARCHIVE_PC, replay-drain pc=$REPLAY_PC"
151	  if [ "$ARCHIVE_PC" = "$REPLAY_PC" ]; then
152	    echo "[demo M6+M7] WARN: replay PC equals archive PC (no progress?)"
153	  fi
154	fi
155	
156	echo "[demo M6+M7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): snap/0 archived + replayed under $RESUME_WITH"
157

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T6: archive emit (M6) + checkpoint replay (M7).
#
# Step A (M6): orchestrator runs a single-spawn QEMU plan that drains
#   on the CSR-enter trigger and archives state.bin + mem + meta.json
#   under $WORKDIR/snap/0/.
# Step B (M7): orchestrator replay-checkpoint hydrates $WORKDIR/snap/0/
#   into a fresh /dev/shm mmap and resumes the chosen model (vsim by
#   default), proving the archive is portable across sessions.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      C-level Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for snap + replay state (default: mktemp -d)
#   RESUME_WITH   vsim | qemu (default: vsim)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-archive-XXXXXX)}"
RESUME_WITH="${RESUME_WITH:-vsim}"

MODE_LABEL="Archive emit (M6) + checkpoint replay (M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs"
echo "Step A: orchestrator run plan -> snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}"
echo "Step B: orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with $RESUME_WITH"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "[demo M6+M7] SKIP: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo M6+M7] SKIP: python3 not found"
  exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
PLAN_YAML="$WORKDIR/plan_archive.yaml"
ARCHIVE_ROOT="$WORKDIR"
RUN_WORKDIR="$WORKDIR/run"
REPLAY_WORKDIR="$WORKDIR/replay"
RUN_LOG="$WORKDIR/run.log"
REPLAY_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay.log"

# Single-step plan: QEMU drains on CSR-enter and archives.
cat >"$PLAN_YAML" <<EOF
session:
  mem_size: 134217728
  mmap_path: null
  elf: hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf

steps:
  - model: qemu
    trigger: { kind: csr }
    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }
EOF

echo "[demo M6+M7 1/4] orchestrator run: drain + archive"
mkdir -p "$RUN_WORKDIR"
set +e
PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
  python3 -m orchestrator.main run "$PLAN_YAML" \
    --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" \
    --workdir "$RUN_WORKDIR" \
    --archive-root "$ARCHIVE_ROOT" \
    >"$RUN_LOG" 2>&1
RUN_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RUN_RC" -ne 200 ] && [ "$RUN_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: orchestrator run exited $RUN_RC"
  tail -30 "$RUN_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

SNAP_DIR="$ARCHIVE_ROOT/snap/0"
for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
  if [ ! -s "$SNAP_DIR/$f" ]; then
    echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: missing or empty $SNAP_DIR/$f"
    ls -la "$SNAP_DIR" 2>&1 || true
    tail -30 "$RUN_LOG"
    exit 1
  fi
done

echo "[demo M6+M7 2/4] validate meta.json schema"
META_OK=$(python3 - "$SNAP_DIR/meta.json" <<'PY'
import json, sys
meta = json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))
assert meta.get("schema") == 1, f"schema {meta.get('schema')!r}"
assert meta.get("abi_version") == 1, f"abi_version {meta.get('abi_version')!r}"
assert meta.get("abi_magic", "").lower() in ("0x42594841", "42594841"), \
    f"abi_magic {meta.get('abi_magic')!r}"
assert meta.get("mem_size") == 134217728, f"mem_size {meta.get('mem_size')!r}"
print("schema=1 abi_version=1 abi_magic=0x42594841 mem_size=128MB")
PY
)
echo "  $META_OK"

echo "[demo M6+M7 3/4] orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with $RESUME_WITH"
mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"
set +e
PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
  python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP_DIR" \
    --resume-with "$RESUME_WITH" \
    --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" \
    --workdir "$REPLAY_WORKDIR" \
    >"$REPLAY_LOG" 2>&1
REPLAY_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$REPLAY_RC" -ne 0 ] && [ "$REPLAY_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M6+M7] FAIL: replay-checkpoint exited $REPLAY_RC"
  tail -30 "$REPLAY_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo M6+M7 4/4] verify replay advanced PC past archive"
REPLAY_STATE=""
for cand in "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"/*state*.bin "$REPLAY_WORKDIR"/*.bin; do
  if [ -s "$cand" ]; then
    REPLAY_STATE="$cand"
    break
  fi
done
if [ -z "$REPLAY_STATE" ]; then
  echo "  no post-replay state file produced (replay completed at exit; OK)"
else
  ARCHIVE_PC=$(python3 -c "
import struct
print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('$SNAP_DIR/state.bin', 'rb').read()[16:24])[0]))")
  REPLAY_PC=$(python3 -c "
import struct
print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('$REPLAY_STATE', 'rb').read()[16:24])[0]))")
  echo "  archive pc=$ARCHIVE_PC, replay-drain pc=$REPLAY_PC"
  if [ "$ARCHIVE_PC" = "$REPLAY_PC" ]; then
    echo "[demo M6+M7] WARN: replay PC equals archive PC (no progress?)"
  fi
fi

echo "[demo M6+M7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): snap/0 archived + replayed under $RESUME_WITH"

1	/* Tier-2 C-level fixture: 1D 3-tap FIR convolution split across all three
2	 * handoff phases, with the V regfile as the carrier of state between
3	 * QEMU 1 <-> vsim and vsim <-> QEMU 2.
4	 *
5	 * Workload
6	 *   y[i] = x[i-1] + 2 * x[i] + x[i+1]     (zero-padded at the edges)
7	 *   x[i] = i + 1                          (i in 0..15)
8	 *
9	 * Phase split (mirrors a realistic hybrid-sim use case: fast functional
10	 * setup / cycle-accurate vector kernel / fast functional verify):
11	 *
12	 *   QEMU 1   generate x via vid + 1
13	 *   vsim     apply the FIR kernel: vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd
14	 *   QEMU 2   vse32 y, scalar verify against the lane-wise closed form
15	 *
16	 * V regfile transport
17	 *   x rides QEMU 1 -> vsim in its compiler-picked V register.
18	 *   y rides vsim   -> QEMU 2 in its compiler-picked V register.
19	 *
20	 *   The fixture never names a physical V register. The runtime helpers
21	 *   v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all (see v_regfile_xfer.h)
22	 *   save/restore the full 32-V-reg regfile bit-identically through
23	 *   V_SCRATCH at each boundary. Because every V reg's contents survive
24	 *   the round trip, the compiler's per-variable tracking ("x lives in
25	 *   v_compiler_choice") stays valid across enter_vsim and exit_vsim and
26	 *   the body of main() can be written in pure RVV intrinsics.
27	 *
28	 * Compiler hazard guard
29	 *   The empty asm volatile blocks with "vr" inputs ("force-live") pin
30	 *   the intrinsic-typed values in V regs at exact source points right
31	 *   before the boundary. Without them, the compiler may elide x's
32	 *   compute from phase 1 (treating it as dead before enter_vsim and
33	 *   re-emittable later) and the plugin would drain a wrong V regfile.
34	 *
35	 * VLEN contract: VLEN=512, e32 m1 -> 16 lanes per V reg. N=16.
36	 *
37	 * Phase-2 rule: no syscalls between enter_vsim and exit_vsim (vsim does
38	 * not implement semihosting).
39	 */
40	#include "runtime/rt_c_helpers.h"
41	#include "runtime/v_regfile_xfer.h"
42	
43	#include <riscv_vector.h>
44	#include <stdint.h>
45	#include <stdio.h>
46	
47	#define N 16
48	
49	int main(void)
50	{
51	  const size_t vl = __riscv_vsetvl_e32m1((size_t) N);
52	
53	  /* Phase 1 (QEMU 1): x = [1, 2, 3, ..., N] via vid + 1. */
54	  const vuint32m1_t vid_u = __riscv_vid_v_u32m1(vl);
55	  const vint32m1_t  x     = __riscv_vadd_vx_i32m1(
56	        __riscv_vreinterpret_v_u32m1_i32m1(vid_u), 1, vl);
57	
58	  asm volatile("" : : "vr"(x));  /* force x live before drain */
59	
60	  enter_vsim();
61	  v_regfile_reload_all();
62	
63	  /* Phase 2 (vsim): 3-tap FIR convolution
64	   *   x_left[i]  = (i == 0)     ? 0 : x[i - 1]   (vslide1up)
65	   *   x_right[i] = (i == N - 1) ? 0 : x[i + 1]   (vslide1down)
66	   *   y[i]       = x_left[i] + 2 * x[i] + x_right[i] */
67	  const vint32m1_t x_left  = __riscv_vslide1up_vx_i32m1(x, 0, vl);
68	  const vint32m1_t x_right = __riscv_vslide1down_vx_i32m1(x, 0, vl);
69	  const vint32m1_t two_x   = __riscv_vsll_vx_i32m1(x, 1, vl);          /* 2*x */
70	  const vint32m1_t partial = __riscv_vadd_vv_i32m1(x_left, two_x, vl); /* x_left + 2x */
71	  const vint32m1_t y       = __riscv_vadd_vv_i32m1(partial, x_right, vl);
72	
73	  asm volatile("" : : "vr"(y));  /* force y live before drain */
74	
75	  v_regfile_dump_all();
76	  exit_vsim();
77	
78	  /* Phase 3 (QEMU 2): store y and verify. */
79	  int32_t out[N];
80	  __riscv_vse32_v_i32m1(out, y, vl);
81	
82	  for (int i = 0; i < N; i++) {
83	    const int32_t x_im1 = (i == 0)     ? 0 : i;        /* x[i-1] zero-padded */
84	    const int32_t x_i   = i + 1;                        /* x[i] */
85	    const int32_t x_ip1 = (i == N - 1) ? 0 : (i + 2);   /* x[i+1] zero-padded */
86	    const int32_t expected = x_im1 + 2 * x_i + x_ip1;
87	    if (out[i] != expected) {
88	      printf("rt_c_v_regs: FAIL i=%d got=%d expected=%d\n", i, (int) out[i],
89	             (int) expected);
90	      fflush(stdout);
91	      return 1;
92	    }
93	  }
94	  printf("rt_c_v_regs: PASS\n");
95	  fflush(stdout);
96	  return 0;
97	}
98

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Build SimPoint 3.2 (tools/simpoint submodule). Produces
3	# tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint - the clustering binary consumed by the
4	# F7/F11 SimPoint pipeline. The test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh ctest discovers
5	# the binary via $SIMPOINT_BIN (defaults to tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint).
6	set -euo pipefail
7	
8	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
9	SRC="$ROOT/tools/simpoint"
10	
11	if [[ ! -f "$SRC/Makefile" ]]; then
12	  echo "missing $SRC/Makefile - run 'git submodule update --init tools/simpoint'" >&2
13	  exit 1
14	fi
15	
16	# The shipped Makefile drives make in analysiscode/ and copies the binary
17	# into bin/. SimPoint 3.2 builds cleanly under modern g++ (verified on
18	# 14.2.1) with no patches.
19	make -C "$SRC" Simpoint
20	
21	# Idempotency guard: the upstream Makefile only invokes `cp ../bin/.` as
22	# part of the link recipe, so on a no-op rebuild bin/simpoint won't be
23	# refreshed. Force-copy from analysiscode/ so the canonical install path
24	# is always populated.
25	install -m 0755 "$SRC/analysiscode/simpoint" "$SRC/bin/simpoint"
26	
27	echo "built: $SRC/bin/simpoint"
28

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build SimPoint 3.2 (tools/simpoint submodule). Produces
# tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint - the clustering binary consumed by the
# F7/F11 SimPoint pipeline. The test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh ctest discovers
# the binary via $SIMPOINT_BIN (defaults to tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint).
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SRC="$ROOT/tools/simpoint"

if [[ ! -f "$SRC/Makefile" ]]; then
  echo "missing $SRC/Makefile - run 'git submodule update --init tools/simpoint'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# The shipped Makefile drives make in analysiscode/ and copies the binary
# into bin/. SimPoint 3.2 builds cleanly under modern g++ (verified on
# 14.2.1) with no patches.
make -C "$SRC" Simpoint

# Idempotency guard: the upstream Makefile only invokes `cp ../bin/.` as
# part of the link recipe, so on a no-op rebuild bin/simpoint won't be
# refreshed. Force-copy from analysiscode/ so the canonical install path
# is always populated.
install -m 0755 "$SRC/analysiscode/simpoint" "$SRC/bin/simpoint"

echo "built: $SRC/bin/simpoint"

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
3	#
4	# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
5	# SimPoint flow:
6	#
7	#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
8	#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
9	#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
10	#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
11	#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
12	#
13	# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
14	# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
15	# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
16	#
17	# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
18	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
19	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
20	#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
21	#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
22	#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
23	#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
24	#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
25	#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
26	#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
27	#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)
28	
29	set -euo pipefail
30	
31	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
32	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
33	TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)
34	
35	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
36	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
37	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
38	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
39	SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
40	WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
41	SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
42	PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
43	PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
44	MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
45	# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
46	# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
47	# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.
48	
49	MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
50	echo "=========================================================="
51	echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
52	echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
53	echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
54	echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
55	echo "=========================================================="
56	
57	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
58	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
59	done
60	if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
61	  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
62	fi
63	if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
64	  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
65	fi
66	
67	mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
68	BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
69	SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
70	WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
71	SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
72	SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
73	
74	# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
75	echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
76	SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
77	PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
78	PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
79	truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
80	trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-20}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-2000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"
# Defaults are tuned for rt_c_v_regs which reaches csrwi 0x7C0,0 after
# ~200-400 insns of QEMU1 startup. slice=20 gives ~15 BBV rows, enough
# for simpoint to find 2 clusters. Tune via env for other fixtures.

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

input.content:
# Hybrid Simulator User Guide

Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
not study its internals. After this guide you can:

1. Build every artifact from a fresh clone.
2. Configure QEMU, vsim, and the orchestrator for a workload.
3. Pick a switching mode (M1..M8) and run one round-trip end-to-end
   with a concrete worked example.

For internals, read `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`. For per-mode drill-down,
read `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`.

---

## 0. The one-paragraph mental model

QEMU runs the boring parts at MHz. vsim (Verilator + SystemC) runs the
small region-of-interest at cycle accuracy. They share RAM via an
`mmap` file. A magic trigger (CSR write, PC, or instruction count)
hands control between them. One run is three legs:

```
   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
```

The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
mode".

---

## 1. Build and environment setup

### 1.1 Host prerequisites

Install these on the host once:

| Package          | Notes                                                |
|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `git`            | clone the repo and its submodules                    |
| C/C++ toolchain  | clang or gcc; needed by QEMU and the plugin          |
| `cmake` >= 3.20  | vsim build                                           |
| `ninja`          | vsim default generator                               |
| `python3`        | orchestrator, plugin tests, demo scripts             |
| `glib-2.0` dev   | QEMU plugin link line                                |
| `pkg-config`     | QEMU configure                                       |
| `podman`         | runs vsim builds inside `vsim-env:latest` container  |
| Verilator        | auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed |
| QEMU's own deps  | pixman, slirp, ... see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`      |
| RISC-V toolchain | `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,nm,objdump}` (Andes v5d)   |

The Andes RISC-V toolchain is needed to build the fixtures and to
resolve symbols for PC-mode. The harness scripts default to
`/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/.../bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`;
override with `NM=/your/path/to/nm` if you keep it elsewhere.

### 1.2 Clone with submodules

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
```

If you forgot `--recursive`:

```sh
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

Submodules under this repo:

| Path             | What it is                                          |
|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `hybrid_qemu/`   | QEMU 9.x fork that hosts the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`) |
| `hybrid_vsim/`   | vsim sources (CMake + SystemC + Verilator)          |
| `tools/simpoint/`| SimPoint 3.2 clustering binary (UCSD)               |

### 1.3 Build everything

One command builds all four artifacts and exports the env vars the
test harness expects:

```sh
source ./setup.sh
```

`source` (not `bash`) is required so the script can prepend the build
dirs to PATH and export `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`,
`SIMPOINT_BIN` into the current shell. The steps it runs:

```
1/4 QEMU       -> build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
2/4 vsim       -> hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium    (in container)
3/4 plugin     -> qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
4/4 SimPoint   -> tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
```

To rebuild just one component:

```sh
./build_qemu.sh
./build_vsim.sh        # uses podman + Dockerfile (target `env`)
make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./hybrid_qemu
./build_simpoint.sh
```

To clean:

```sh
source ./setup.sh clean         # all four
source ./setup.sh clean qemu    # just QEMU build/
source ./setup.sh clean vsim
source ./setup.sh clean plugin
source ./setup.sh clean simpoint
```

### 1.4 Build the test fixtures (RISC-V ELFs)

Fixtures are small RISC-V programs that exercise specific transport
paths. They are NOT built by `setup.sh`; build them once:

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test -j
```

One fixture at a time:

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test rt_c_v_regs.elf
```

Output ELFs land next to the sources under
`hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/*.elf`. The fixtures depend on the Andes
RISC-V GCC + the `libgloss_vh` semihosting library. If your toolchain
path differs from the Makefile defaults, edit `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile`
or override `CC`, `LD`, `AR`, `OBJCOPY` on the make command line.

### 1.5 Sanity check the build

```sh
# All four artifacts present?
ls build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
ls hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium
ls qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
ls tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint

# Smallest possible end-to-end - one CSR-mode round-trip:
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[rt_c_v_regs 3/3 mode=csr] PASS: round-trip exit code 0
```

If you see `skip: ... not built`, redo `source ./setup.sh` and
re-build fixtures (`make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test`).

---

## 2. Configuring the simulator

There are four configuration surfaces. Most users only touch the env
vars in 2.1.

### 2.1 Environment variables (the only ones you usually set)

`setup.sh` exports these for you. Override only if your artifacts live
somewhere non-default.

| Variable        | Default                                          | Used by                  |
|-----------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|
| `VSIM_QEMU`     | `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`                 | every harness            |
| `VSIM_PLUGIN`   | `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`               | every harness            |
| `VSIM_BIN`      | first `hybrid_vsim/build/sim_*`                  | every harness            |
| `VSIM_ELF`      | `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/$FIXTURE.elf`           | harness scripts          |
| `SIMPOINT_BIN`  | `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`                    | M4/T7 demos              |
| `NM`            | Andes toolchain `nm`                             | PC-mode symbol resolve   |
| `TIMEOUT`       | 60 s (some demos use 120)                        | per-leg `timeout` cap    |
| `FIXTURE`       | `rt_shared_mem`                                  | which `.elf` to load     |
| `EXPECT_RC`     | 0                                                | expected QEMU2 exit code |
| `TRIGGER_MODE`  | `csr`                                            | `csr` or `pc`            |
| `JOBS`          | auto-cap on this 128-core host: set to 8         | parallel test runner     |

Example: override the QEMU binary for one run:

```sh
VSIM_QEMU=/opt/qemu-9.0/qemu-system-riscv64 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
```

### 2.2 The vsim CPU configuration YAML

`hybrid_vsim/config.yaml` selects which Andes core to verilate. The
default is `ax45mpv_premium`:

```yaml
cpus:
  ax45mpv_premium:
    cpu_series: 45
    rtl_package: ax45mpv_premium
    cpp_wrapper: ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem
    config: data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg
    config_dir: andes_ip/kv_core/top/hdl
    config_inc_filter: grep '^`define NDS_'
    extra_verilator_flags: --threads 8 --unroll-count 256
```

Switch CPU by pointing CMake at a different YAML:

```sh
cd hybrid_vsim
cmake -S . -B build -G Ninja -DVSIM_CONFIG_FILE=my_other_cpu.yaml
cmake --build build -j
```

`-DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON` registers the e2e fixtures with CTest;
`-DVSIM_NO_VERILATOR=ON` skips Verilator (use a prebuilt model).

### 2.3 QEMU plugin args

The plugin reads `arg=value` tokens after `-plugin <path>,`. They
select WHICH switching mode QEMU1 runs in:

| Plugin arg           | Effect                                                                 |
|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `outfile=PATH`       | REQUIRED. Drained `hybrid_state_v1` is written here.                   |
| `enter_pc=0xADDR`    | M2: drain when PC == ADDR. Bypasses the CSR-insn matcher.              |
| `icount=N`           | M3: drain on the N-th retired insn (per vcpu).                         |
| `bbv_out=PATH`       | M4: emit one SimPoint BBV row per `slice=N` insns into PATH.           |
| `slice=N`            | Insns per slice (used by M4 and M5). Must be > 0.                      |
| `slice_at=K`         | M5: drain at the end of the K-th slice. Requires `slice=N`.            |

If no mode arg is given, the default is CSR-insn match (M1): the
plugin matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` at retire.

Example invocation (CSR mode, just the minimal arg):

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin
```

Example (icount mode):

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin,icount=10000
```

### 2.4 vsim CLI flags

`sim_ax45mpv_premium <elf> [flags]`. The hybrid-relevant subset:

| Flag                          | Purpose                                                  |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `--resume-from-qemu FILE`     | Read drained state, restore into hart, run from PC.     |
| `--handoff-out FILE`          | Drain back into FILE when the exit trigger fires.       |
| `--shared-mem-path FILE`      | mmap FILE as RAM (zero-copy share with QEMU).           |
| `--shared-mem-size BYTES`     | Size of the shared region. Match QEMU `-m`.             |
| `--shared-mem-base HEX`       | Guest physical address where file offset 0 lands.       |
| `--qemu-binary PATH`          | After drain, spawn this QEMU as Phase 3 (QEMU2).        |
| `--hybrid-exit-pc 0xADDR`     | M2 exit: drain when this PC retires.                    |
| `--hybrid-icount N`           | M3 exit: drain after N committed insns (vsim side).     |
| `--jtag-port N`               | Open the JTAG bitbang port (e.g. OpenOCD).              |
| `--log-level N`               | 0=trace .. 5=critical .. 6=off (default 2=info)         |

Defaults for `--shared-mem-base` is `0x80000000` (the QEMU `virt` /
`andes_ae350` RAM base). Most users never override it.

Run `$VSIM_BIN --help` for the full list (waveform/save/restore are
license-gated and off by default).

### 2.5 Orchestrator plan YAML (used by M6/M7)

For checkpoint workflows you describe a multi-step plan in YAML and
hand it to the orchestrator:

```yaml
session:
  mem_size: 134217728            # 128 MB shared mmap
  mmap_path: null                # null -> mktemp in /dev/shm
  elf: hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf

steps:
  - model: qemu
    trigger: { kind: csr }       # M1 trigger; can also be { kind: pc, enter: 0x... } or { kind: icount, n: 5000 }
    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }
```

Run it:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
  --project-root . --workdir /tmp/run --archive-root /tmp/archives
```

Replay:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/archives/snap/0 \
  --resume-with vsim --project-root . --workdir /tmp/replay
```

---

## 3. Switching modes - which trigger to use

| ID  | Trigger                                | Where        | When to pick it                                                |
|-----|----------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| M1  | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1` insn match          | QEMU1 + vsim | Default. Your binary can embed `csrwi` markers.                |
| M2  | PC equals `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc`    | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is yours, but you cannot insert CSR markers.            |
| M3  | Retired-instruction count              | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is NOT yours (no CSR/symbol). Use a spin loop / blob.   |
| M4  | BBV emit + offline SimPoint cluster    | QEMU1 only   | Phase identification before the real run.                      |
| M5  | Drain at end of slice K                | QEMU1        | Replay one cluster representative slice on vsim.               |
| M6  | Archive state.bin + mem + meta after drain | orchestrator | Capture a portable, shippable checkpoint.                     |
| M7  | Replay a snap/K archive                | orchestrator | Resume the same checkpoint hours / days / hosts later.         |
| M8  | QEMU2 exits naturally via semihosting  | QEMU2        | Always. End-of-program signal.                                 |

M1/M2/M3 are the three ways to delimit the ROI. M4..M7 stack on top
of them for SimPoint-style phase replay. M8 is automatic.

Decision flow:

```
Can you edit the binary?
  yes -> insert csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1 around the ROI -> M1
  no  -> Does it export _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc?
           yes -> M2
           no  -> Use icount  -> M3
You want phase-aware replay?  -> M4 + M5 + M6 + M7   (the T7 demo automates this)
```

---

## 4. Walk-through: one round-trip in each mode

Each section is copy-pastable. They all share the same setup.

### 4.0 Common setup (do once per shell)

```sh
cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim
source ./setup.sh                         # builds + exports env vars
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test -j        # builds .elf fixtures
```

Quick env check:

```sh
echo "$VSIM_QEMU"
echo "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
echo "$VSIM_BIN"
ls hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf
```

### 4.1 M1 - CSR insn match (the default)

Goal: run a vector fixture through QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 using the
two `csrwi 0x7C0, *` markers that the fixture already contains.

```sh
bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[demo M1] PASS (CSR insn match (M1+M8))
```

The demo is a thin wrapper around the generic harness:

```sh
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
```

Inside, the three legs are:

```
1) QEMU1 with -plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE
        -> drain at csrwi 0x7C0,0   -> exit 200
2) sim_ax45mpv_premium $ELF \
        --resume-from-qemu $STATE --handoff-out $STATE_V \
        --shared-mem-path $SHM --shared-mem-size 128MB \
        --qemu-binary $VSIM_QEMU
        -> ROI runs in vsim, drains at csrwi 0x7C0,1, spawns QEMU2
3) QEMU2 (spawned by vsim) restores state via gdbstub, continues,
        exits 0 via semihosting (M8).
```

Try a different fixture:

```sh
FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
```

### 4.2 M2 - PC symbol resolve

Same workload, but the trigger is a PC value from the symbol table.

```sh
bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
```

To see what the demo passes under the hood:

```sh
NM=/path/to/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm   # default in $NM if you ran setup.sh

ENTER_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
EXIT_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')

echo "enter=$ENTER_PC  exit=$EXIT_PC"

# QEMU1 leg
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/share,size=134217728 \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=/tmp/q1.bin,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"

# vsim leg
"$VSIM_BIN" hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
  --resume-from-qemu /tmp/q1.bin \
  --handoff-out /tmp/v.bin \
  --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/share \
  --shared-mem-size 134217728 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  --hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC"
```

The fixture binary is unchanged from M1; only the orchestrator
arguments are different.

### 4.3 M3 - icount round-trip (no markers)

For workloads you cannot edit. Both legs drain on a retired-insn count
target.

```sh
bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
```

Override the targets:

```sh
QEMU_ICOUNT=20000 VSIM_ICOUNT=8000 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
```

What you can verify by hand: the demo extracts the drained PC from
`state.bin` at byte offset 16, little-endian u64. For `icount_spin.elf`
the loop is two RVC insns at `0x100002` and `0x100004`:

```sh
python3 -c "
import struct
print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('/tmp/icount_state.bin','rb').read()[16:24])[0]))
"
```

### 4.4 M4 - SimPoint BBV profile pass

QEMU1 emits one BBV row per slice; the offline `simpoint` tool
clusters them. No vsim leg.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh
```

Plugin args under the hood:

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,bbv_out=$WORKDIR/profile.bb,slice=1000,icount=10000
```

That writes 10 BBV rows (`icount / slice`). Then:

```
simpoint -loadFVFile $WORKDIR/profile.bb -maxK 2 -dim 2 \
         -saveSimpoints $WORKDIR/simpts \
         -saveSimpointWeights $WORKDIR/weights
```

`simpts` lines: `<slice_idx> <cluster_id>`. `weights` lines:
`<mass> <cluster_id>`. Tune `SLICE`, `ICOUNT`, `MAXK` via env vars.

### 4.5 M5 - slice-consume replay entry

Given a `K` chosen from M4's `simpts` output, drain QEMU at the END of
slice K so vsim resumes from exactly that point.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh
SIMPTS=/tmp/m4-profile/simpts \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
```

Manual K:

```sh
SLICE=1000 SLICE_AT=3 bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
```

Plugin args:

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,slice=1000,slice_at=3,icount=10000
```

`icount=` is a safety fallback if K is invalid.

### 4.6 M6 + M7 - archive checkpoint and replay

Capture a portable archive `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` from a
single QEMU drain, then resume it on vsim (or QEMU) in a separate
process.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7 bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh
```

Replay on QEMU instead of vsim:

```sh
RESUME_WITH=qemu WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7-qemu \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh
```

The orchestrator commands you can run by hand:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
  --project-root . \
  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/run \
  --archive-root /tmp/m6m7
# -> /tmp/m6m7/snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/m6m7/snap/0 \
  --resume-with vsim \
  --project-root . \
  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/replay
```

### 4.7 T7 - the centerpiece (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)

One command chains all four phases on `rt_c_v_regs.elf`:

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/t7 bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[demo T7] PASS (Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)): bbv_rows=15 phases=2 replays_ok=2 workdir=/tmp/t7
```

To run a different fixture, set `VSIM_ELF` and tune `SLICE` so the
BBV pass yields 10..30 rows:

```sh
VSIM_ELF=hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_matmul.elf \
  SLICE=50 PROFILE_ICOUNT=4000 MAXK=3 \
  WORKDIR=/tmp/t7-matmul \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
```

---

## 5. Running the full test suite

After any change to the simulator or plugin, run the suite:

```sh
JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh
```

`JOBS=8` is the practical cap on this 128-core host (vsim Verilator
processes are CPU-hungry; the default cap of 128 saturates the box and
times out). Logs go to `/tmp/e2e-<RAND>/`; failed-test logs are
streamed at the end.

Run a subset:

```sh
FILTER=demo  JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh         # only the M1..M8 demos
FILTER=rt_c_v JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh        # only V-related fixtures
MODE=csr     JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh         # skip the pc-mode iterations
JOBS=1                     bash run_e2e.sh  # sequential for debugging
LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs       bash run_e2e.sh
```

Single-test sequential demo runner (one banner per test):

```sh
cd hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test
make demo
# or
FILTER='csr|pc' bash demo.sh
```

CTest entry per demo:

```sh
ctest --test-dir hybrid_vsim/build -L switching-mode --output-on-failure
ctest --test-dir hybrid_vsim/build -R demo_csr      # one demo
```

---

## 6. Common failures and how to read them

| Symptom                                                | Cause / fix                                                          |
|--------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `skip: ... not built; run 'make' ...`                  | `source ./setup.sh` and `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test`            |
| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 0`                      | Trigger never fired. Wrong mode / wrong PC / wrong icount value.     |
| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 124`                    | `timeout` killed the plugin. Increase `TIMEOUT` or tune the target.  |
| `missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols`     | M2 needs a fixture with those labels. Rebuild or use M1.             |
| `vsim drained pc=0x... not in icount_spin loop`        | `VSIM_ICOUNT` too small / wrong fixture for M3.                      |
| Whole suite times out under `run_e2e.sh`               | Set `JOBS=8`. Default 128 saturates a 128-core host.                 |
| `[FAIL] [N/M] ... -> /tmp/e2e-XXXX/...log`             | Open that log; it is the full stdout+stderr of the failing leg.      |

---

## 7. Where to go next

- `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` - architecture deep-dive (state ABI,
  Debug Module transport, V-scratch trick, passthrough rationale).
- `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md` - one exercise per M1..M8 with
  drill-down on the underlying CLI invocations.
- `CLAUDE.md` - file-level pointers (key files, build commands,
  fixture authoring rules).
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c` - reference fixture
  combining V regfile + V scalar CSRs + shared memory.

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/docs/USER_GUIDE.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

toolUseResult.content:
# Hybrid Simulator User Guide

Audience: a new engineer who wants to RUN the Andes hybrid simulator,
not study its internals. After this guide you can:

1. Build every artifact from a fresh clone.
2. Configure QEMU, vsim, and the orchestrator for a workload.
3. Pick a switching mode (M1..M8) and run one round-trip end-to-end
   with a concrete worked example.

For internals, read `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md`. For per-mode drill-down,
read `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md`.

---

## 0. The one-paragraph mental model

QEMU runs the boring parts at MHz. vsim (Verilator + SystemC) runs the
small region-of-interest at cycle accuracy. They share RAM via an
`mmap` file. A magic trigger (CSR write, PC, or instruction count)
hands control between them. One run is three legs:

```
   QEMU1  -- state.bin + shared mmap -->  vsim  -- same files -->  QEMU2
   (fast)                                 (cycle accurate)         (fast)
```

The thing you choose per run is the TRIGGER that decides where each
leg drains and the next leg resumes. That choice is the "switching
mode".

---

## 1. Build and environment setup

### 1.1 Host prerequisites

Install these on the host once:

| Package          | Notes                                                |
|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `git`            | clone the repo and its submodules                    |
| C/C++ toolchain  | clang or gcc; needed by QEMU and the plugin          |
| `cmake` >= 3.20  | vsim build                                           |
| `ninja`          | vsim default generator                               |
| `python3`        | orchestrator, plugin tests, demo scripts             |
| `glib-2.0` dev   | QEMU plugin link line                                |
| `pkg-config`     | QEMU configure                                       |
| `podman`         | runs vsim builds inside `vsim-env:latest` container  |
| Verilator        | auto-detected; set `VSIM_VERILATOR_EXECUTABLE` if needed |
| QEMU's own deps  | pixman, slirp, ... see `hybrid_qemu/README.rst`      |
| RISC-V toolchain | `riscv64-unknown-elf-{gcc,nm,objdump}` (Andes v5d)   |

The Andes RISC-V toolchain is needed to build the fixtures and to
resolve symbols for PC-mode. The harness scripts default to
`/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/.../bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm`;
override with `NM=/your/path/to/nm` if you keep it elsewhere.

### 1.2 Clone with submodules

```sh
git clone --recursive https://gitea.andestech.com/nick/hybrid_sim
cd hybrid_sim
```

If you forgot `--recursive`:

```sh
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

Submodules under this repo:

| Path             | What it is                                          |
|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| `hybrid_qemu/`   | QEMU 9.x fork that hosts the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`) |
| `hybrid_vsim/`   | vsim sources (CMake + SystemC + Verilator)          |
| `tools/simpoint/`| SimPoint 3.2 clustering binary (UCSD)               |

### 1.3 Build everything

One command builds all four artifacts and exports the env vars the
test harness expects:

```sh
source ./setup.sh
```

`source` (not `bash`) is required so the script can prepend the build
dirs to PATH and export `VSIM_QEMU`, `VSIM_PLUGIN`, `VSIM_BIN`,
`SIMPOINT_BIN` into the current shell. The steps it runs:

```
1/4 QEMU       -> build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
2/4 vsim       -> hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium    (in container)
3/4 plugin     -> qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
4/4 SimPoint   -> tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
```

To rebuild just one component:

```sh
./build_qemu.sh
./build_vsim.sh        # uses podman + Dockerfile (target `env`)
make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./hybrid_qemu
./build_simpoint.sh
```

To clean:

```sh
source ./setup.sh clean         # all four
source ./setup.sh clean qemu    # just QEMU build/
source ./setup.sh clean vsim
source ./setup.sh clean plugin
source ./setup.sh clean simpoint
```

### 1.4 Build the test fixtures (RISC-V ELFs)

Fixtures are small RISC-V programs that exercise specific transport
paths. They are NOT built by `setup.sh`; build them once:

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test -j
```

One fixture at a time:

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test rt_c_v_regs.elf
```

Output ELFs land next to the sources under
`hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/*.elf`. The fixtures depend on the Andes
RISC-V GCC + the `libgloss_vh` semihosting library. If your toolchain
path differs from the Makefile defaults, edit `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile`
or override `CC`, `LD`, `AR`, `OBJCOPY` on the make command line.

### 1.5 Sanity check the build

```sh
# All four artifacts present?
ls build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
ls hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium
ls qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
ls tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint

# Smallest possible end-to-end - one CSR-mode round-trip:
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[rt_c_v_regs 3/3 mode=csr] PASS: round-trip exit code 0
```

If you see `skip: ... not built`, redo `source ./setup.sh` and
re-build fixtures (`make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test`).

---

## 2. Configuring the simulator

There are four configuration surfaces. Most users only touch the env
vars in 2.1.

### 2.1 Environment variables (the only ones you usually set)

`setup.sh` exports these for you. Override only if your artifacts live
somewhere non-default.

| Variable        | Default                                          | Used by                  |
|-----------------|--------------------------------------------------|--------------------------|
| `VSIM_QEMU`     | `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`                 | every harness            |
| `VSIM_PLUGIN`   | `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`               | every harness            |
| `VSIM_BIN`      | first `hybrid_vsim/build/sim_*`                  | every harness            |
| `VSIM_ELF`      | `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/$FIXTURE.elf`           | harness scripts          |
| `SIMPOINT_BIN`  | `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`                    | M4/T7 demos              |
| `NM`            | Andes toolchain `nm`                             | PC-mode symbol resolve   |
| `TIMEOUT`       | 60 s (some demos use 120)                        | per-leg `timeout` cap    |
| `FIXTURE`       | `rt_shared_mem`                                  | which `.elf` to load     |
| `EXPECT_RC`     | 0                                                | expected QEMU2 exit code |
| `TRIGGER_MODE`  | `csr`                                            | `csr` or `pc`            |
| `JOBS`          | auto-cap on this 128-core host: set to 8         | parallel test runner     |

Example: override the QEMU binary for one run:

```sh
VSIM_QEMU=/opt/qemu-9.0/qemu-system-riscv64 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
```

### 2.2 The vsim CPU configuration YAML

`hybrid_vsim/config.yaml` selects which Andes core to verilate. The
default is `ax45mpv_premium`:

```yaml
cpus:
  ax45mpv_premium:
    cpu_series: 45
    rtl_package: ax45mpv_premium
    cpp_wrapper: ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem
    config: data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg
    config_dir: andes_ip/kv_core/top/hdl
    config_inc_filter: grep '^`define NDS_'
    extra_verilator_flags: --threads 8 --unroll-count 256
```

Switch CPU by pointing CMake at a different YAML:

```sh
cd hybrid_vsim
cmake -S . -B build -G Ninja -DVSIM_CONFIG_FILE=my_other_cpu.yaml
cmake --build build -j
```

`-DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON` registers the e2e fixtures with CTest;
`-DVSIM_NO_VERILATOR=ON` skips Verilator (use a prebuilt model).

### 2.3 QEMU plugin args

The plugin reads `arg=value` tokens after `-plugin <path>,`. They
select WHICH switching mode QEMU1 runs in:

| Plugin arg           | Effect                                                                 |
|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `outfile=PATH`       | REQUIRED. Drained `hybrid_state_v1` is written here.                   |
| `enter_pc=0xADDR`    | M2: drain when PC == ADDR. Bypasses the CSR-insn matcher.              |
| `icount=N`           | M3: drain on the N-th retired insn (per vcpu).                         |
| `bbv_out=PATH`       | M4: emit one SimPoint BBV row per `slice=N` insns into PATH.           |
| `slice=N`            | Insns per slice (used by M4 and M5). Must be > 0.                      |
| `slice_at=K`         | M5: drain at the end of the K-th slice. Requires `slice=N`.            |

If no mode arg is given, the default is CSR-insn match (M1): the
plugin matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` at retire.

Example invocation (CSR mode, just the minimal arg):

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin
```

Example (icount mode):

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=/tmp/state.bin,icount=10000
```

### 2.4 vsim CLI flags

`sim_ax45mpv_premium <elf> [flags]`. The hybrid-relevant subset:

| Flag                          | Purpose                                                  |
|-------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `--resume-from-qemu FILE`     | Read drained state, restore into hart, run from PC.     |
| `--handoff-out FILE`          | Drain back into FILE when the exit trigger fires.       |
| `--shared-mem-path FILE`      | mmap FILE as RAM (zero-copy share with QEMU).           |
| `--shared-mem-size BYTES`     | Size of the shared region. Match QEMU `-m`.             |
| `--shared-mem-base HEX`       | Guest physical address where file offset 0 lands.       |
| `--qemu-binary PATH`          | After drain, spawn this QEMU as Phase 3 (QEMU2).        |
| `--hybrid-exit-pc 0xADDR`     | M2 exit: drain when this PC retires.                    |
| `--hybrid-icount N`           | M3 exit: drain after N committed insns (vsim side).     |
| `--jtag-port N`               | Open the JTAG bitbang port (e.g. OpenOCD).              |
| `--log-level N`               | 0=trace .. 5=critical .. 6=off (default 2=info)         |

Defaults for `--shared-mem-base` is `0x80000000` (the QEMU `virt` /
`andes_ae350` RAM base). Most users never override it.

Run `$VSIM_BIN --help` for the full list (waveform/save/restore are
license-gated and off by default).

### 2.5 Orchestrator plan YAML (used by M6/M7)

For checkpoint workflows you describe a multi-step plan in YAML and
hand it to the orchestrator:

```yaml
session:
  mem_size: 134217728            # 128 MB shared mmap
  mmap_path: null                # null -> mktemp in /dev/shm
  elf: hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf

steps:
  - model: qemu
    trigger: { kind: csr }       # M1 trigger; can also be { kind: pc, enter: 0x... } or { kind: icount, n: 5000 }
    on_exit: { archive_as: "snap/0" }
```

Run it:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
  --project-root . --workdir /tmp/run --archive-root /tmp/archives
```

Replay:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/archives/snap/0 \
  --resume-with vsim --project-root . --workdir /tmp/replay
```

---

## 3. Switching modes - which trigger to use

| ID  | Trigger                                | Where        | When to pick it                                                |
|-----|----------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| M1  | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1` insn match          | QEMU1 + vsim | Default. Your binary can embed `csrwi` markers.                |
| M2  | PC equals `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc`    | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is yours, but you cannot insert CSR markers.            |
| M3  | Retired-instruction count              | QEMU1 + vsim | Binary is NOT yours (no CSR/symbol). Use a spin loop / blob.   |
| M4  | BBV emit + offline SimPoint cluster    | QEMU1 only   | Phase identification before the real run.                      |
| M5  | Drain at end of slice K                | QEMU1        | Replay one cluster representative slice on vsim.               |
| M6  | Archive state.bin + mem + meta after drain | orchestrator | Capture a portable, shippable checkpoint.                     |
| M7  | Replay a snap/K archive                | orchestrator | Resume the same checkpoint hours / days / hosts later.         |
| M8  | QEMU2 exits naturally via semihosting  | QEMU2        | Always. End-of-program signal.                                 |

M1/M2/M3 are the three ways to delimit the ROI. M4..M7 stack on top
of them for SimPoint-style phase replay. M8 is automatic.

Decision flow:

```
Can you edit the binary?
  yes -> insert csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1 around the ROI -> M1
  no  -> Does it export _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc?
           yes -> M2
           no  -> Use icount  -> M3
You want phase-aware replay?  -> M4 + M5 + M6 + M7   (the T7 demo automates this)
```

---

## 4. Walk-through: one round-trip in each mode

Each section is copy-pastable. They all share the same setup.

### 4.0 Common setup (do once per shell)

```sh
cd /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim
source ./setup.sh                         # builds + exports env vars
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test -j        # builds .elf fixtures
```

Quick env check:

```sh
echo "$VSIM_QEMU"
echo "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
echo "$VSIM_BIN"
ls hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf
```

### 4.1 M1 - CSR insn match (the default)

Goal: run a vector fixture through QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 using the
two `csrwi 0x7C0, *` markers that the fixture already contains.

```sh
bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[demo M1] PASS (CSR insn match (M1+M8))
```

The demo is a thin wrapper around the generic harness:

```sh
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
```

Inside, the three legs are:

```
1) QEMU1 with -plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE
        -> drain at csrwi 0x7C0,0   -> exit 200
2) sim_ax45mpv_premium $ELF \
        --resume-from-qemu $STATE --handoff-out $STATE_V \
        --shared-mem-path $SHM --shared-mem-size 128MB \
        --qemu-binary $VSIM_QEMU
        -> ROI runs in vsim, drains at csrwi 0x7C0,1, spawns QEMU2
3) QEMU2 (spawned by vsim) restores state via gdbstub, continues,
        exits 0 via semihosting (M8).
```

Try a different fixture:

```sh
FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh
```

### 4.2 M2 - PC symbol resolve

Same workload, but the trigger is a PC value from the symbol table.

```sh
bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh
```

To see what the demo passes under the hood:

```sh
NM=/path/to/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm   # default in $NM if you ran setup.sh

ENTER_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
EXIT_PC=$("$NM" --defined-only hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
            | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')

echo "enter=$ENTER_PC  exit=$EXIT_PC"

# QEMU1 leg
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/share,size=134217728 \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=/tmp/q1.bin,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"

# vsim leg
"$VSIM_BIN" hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf \
  --resume-from-qemu /tmp/q1.bin \
  --handoff-out /tmp/v.bin \
  --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/share \
  --shared-mem-size 134217728 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  --hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC"
```

The fixture binary is unchanged from M1; only the orchestrator
arguments are different.

### 4.3 M3 - icount round-trip (no markers)

For workloads you cannot edit. Both legs drain on a retired-insn count
target.

```sh
bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
```

Override the targets:

```sh
QEMU_ICOUNT=20000 VSIM_ICOUNT=8000 \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh
```

What you can verify by hand: the demo extracts the drained PC from
`state.bin` at byte offset 16, little-endian u64. For `icount_spin.elf`
the loop is two RVC insns at `0x100002` and `0x100004`:

```sh
python3 -c "
import struct
print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', open('/tmp/icount_state.bin','rb').read()[16:24])[0]))
"
```

### 4.4 M4 - SimPoint BBV profile pass

QEMU1 emits one BBV row per slice; the offline `simpoint` tool
clusters them. No vsim leg.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh
```

Plugin args under the hood:

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,bbv_out=$WORKDIR/profile.bb,slice=1000,icount=10000
```

That writes 10 BBV rows (`icount / slice`). Then:

```
simpoint -loadFVFile $WORKDIR/profile.bb -maxK 2 -dim 2 \
         -saveSimpoints $WORKDIR/simpts \
         -saveSimpointWeights $WORKDIR/weights
```

`simpts` lines: `<slice_idx> <cluster_id>`. `weights` lines:
`<mass> <cluster_id>`. Tune `SLICE`, `ICOUNT`, `MAXK` via env vars.

### 4.5 M5 - slice-consume replay entry

Given a `K` chosen from M4's `simpts` output, drain QEMU at the END of
slice K so vsim resumes from exactly that point.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m4-profile bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh
SIMPTS=/tmp/m4-profile/simpts \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
```

Manual K:

```sh
SLICE=1000 SLICE_AT=3 bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh
```

Plugin args:

```
-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$STATE,slice=1000,slice_at=3,icount=10000
```

`icount=` is a safety fallback if K is invalid.

### 4.6 M6 + M7 - archive checkpoint and replay

Capture a portable archive `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` from a
single QEMU drain, then resume it on vsim (or QEMU) in a separate
process.

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7 bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh
```

Replay on QEMU instead of vsim:

```sh
RESUME_WITH=qemu WORKDIR=/tmp/m6m7-qemu \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh
```

The orchestrator commands you can run by hand:

```sh
python3 -m orchestrator.main run plan.yaml \
  --project-root . \
  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/run \
  --archive-root /tmp/m6m7
# -> /tmp/m6m7/snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint /tmp/m6m7/snap/0 \
  --resume-with vsim \
  --project-root . \
  --workdir /tmp/m6m7/replay
```

### 4.7 T7 - the centerpiece (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)

One command chains all four phases on `rt_c_v_regs.elf`:

```sh
WORKDIR=/tmp/t7 bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
```

Expected last line:

```
[demo T7] PASS (Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)): bbv_rows=15 phases=2 replays_ok=2 workdir=/tmp/t7
```

To run a different fixture, set `VSIM_ELF` and tune `SLICE` so the
BBV pass yields 10..30 rows:

```sh
VSIM_ELF=hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_matmul.elf \
  SLICE=50 PROFILE_ICOUNT=4000 MAXK=3 \
  WORKDIR=/tmp/t7-matmul \
  bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh
```

---

## 5. Running the full test suite

After any change to the simulator or plugin, run the suite:

```sh
JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh
```

`JOBS=8` is the practical cap on this 128-core host (vsim Verilator
processes are CPU-hungry; the default cap of 128 saturates the box and
times out). Logs go to `/tmp/e2e-<RAND>/`; failed-test logs are
streamed at the end.

Run a subset:

```sh
FILTER=demo  JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh         # only the M1..M8 demos
FILTER=rt_c_v JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh        # only V-related fixtures
MODE=csr     JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh         # skip the pc-mode iterations
JOBS=1                     bash run_e2e.sh  # sequential for debugging
LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs       bash run_e2e.sh
```

Single-test sequential demo runner (one banner per test):

```sh
cd hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test
make demo
# or
FILTER='csr|pc' bash demo.sh
```

CTest entry per demo:

```sh
ctest --test-dir hybrid_vsim/build -L switching-mode --output-on-failure
ctest --test-dir hybrid_vsim/build -R demo_csr      # one demo
```

---

## 6. Common failures and how to read them

| Symptom                                                | Cause / fix                                                          |
|--------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `skip: ... not built; run 'make' ...`                  | `source ./setup.sh` and `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test`            |
| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 0`                      | Trigger never fired. Wrong mode / wrong PC / wrong icount value.     |
| `QEMU 1 expected exit 200, got 124`                    | `timeout` killed the plugin. Increase `TIMEOUT` or tune the target.  |
| `missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols`     | M2 needs a fixture with those labels. Rebuild or use M1.             |
| `vsim drained pc=0x... not in icount_spin loop`        | `VSIM_ICOUNT` too small / wrong fixture for M3.                      |
| Whole suite times out under `run_e2e.sh`               | Set `JOBS=8`. Default 128 saturates a 128-core host.                 |
| `[FAIL] [N/M] ... -> /tmp/e2e-XXXX/...log`             | Open that log; it is the full stdout+stderr of the failing leg.      |

---

## 7. Where to go next

- `docs/HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` - architecture deep-dive (state ABI,
  Debug Module transport, V-scratch trick, passthrough rationale).
- `docs/switching_modes_tutorial.md` - one exercise per M1..M8 with
  drill-down on the underlying CLI invocations.
- `CLAUDE.md` - file-level pointers (key files, build commands,
  fixture authoring rules).
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c` - reference fixture
  combining V regfile + V scalar CSRs + shared memory.

content.text:
Wrote `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` (515 lines). Structure:

1. **Build/setup** (sec 1): host deps, submodule clone, `source ./setup.sh`, fixture build, sanity check.
2. **Configuration** (sec 2): env vars, vsim YAML, QEMU plugin args, vsim CLI flags, orchestrator plan YAML.
3. **Switching modes** (sec 3 + 4): mode table M1..M8, decision flow, then one copy-pastable walk-through per mode (M1 CSR, M2 PC, M3 icount, M4 BBV, M5 slice-consume, M6+M7 archive/replay, T7 full pipeline).
4. Supporting sections: full-suite runner, failure signatures, pointers to deeper docs.

Each mode walk-through gives both the one-line demo invocation and the underlying plugin/CLI args so the user can adapt to their own fixture. Concrete example: section 4.2 shows the literal `riscv64-unknown-elf-nm` + `QEMU + plugin + vsim` commands a user would type to drive PC-mode by hand.

file.content:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump demo

all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Note: handoff.elf, handoff_exit.elf, spin.elf are legacy (referenced only
# by EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL handoff_smoke.cpp). They still build for completeness
# but are not in active CTest. All targets now share test.ld at 0x00100000.

# Switching-mode demo runner: builds the fixtures if needed, then walks
# T1..T7 from $(DEMO_DIR), printing a banner per mode and a final
# pass/fail tally. Honors FILTER=<regex> to subset the run.
demo: all
	@bash demo.sh

spin.elf: spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

# CSR-less spin for F5 plugin-icount e2e. Linked at 0x00100000 (test.ld)
# so andes_ae350's MROM at 0x80000000 stays clear when loaded via -bios.
icount_spin.elf: icount_spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c icount_spin.S -o icount_spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) icount_spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x00100000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime + v_regfile_xfer.c, different
# -march. Listed separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally
# drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

content.text:
Hardcoded paths today (17 sites):

```
hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile               TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/.../nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh      NM=...,  VSIM_QEMU=/local/nick/qemu_v5/...
hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh      NM=...,  VSIM_QEMU=/local/nick/qemu_v5/...
hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh             VSIM_QEMU=/local/nick/qemu_v5/...        (5 files)
hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_plugin_*.sh      VSIM_QEMU=/local/nick/qemu_v5/...        (4 files)
hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_{pty,gdbstub,no_qemu_binary}.sh  VSIM_QEMU=...               (3 files)
```

Proposed core design before I touch leaves:

- New file `config.env` at repo root (key=value shell-sourceable). Two vars: `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` (toolchain prefix dir; `$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-*` are the binaries) and optionally `HYBRID_QEMU` (default QEMU path if you don't build the in-tree one).
- `setup.sh` sources `config.env` and exports `HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN` (it already exports `VSIM_QEMU` from the built path).
- `hybrid/test/Makefile`: `TOOLCHAIN ?= $(HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN)`.
- All test scripts: derive `NM` from `${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN}/bin/...` and the `VSIM_QEMU` fallback from `${HYBRID_QEMU}` instead of `/local/nick/...`.

Two open questions before I implement.

file.content:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
