toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim + plugin + simpoint, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh                       # build all
#   source ./setup.sh clean [target]        # clean qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all (default: all)

if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
else
  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
fi

# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
# under nounset).
_hybrid_setup_main() {
  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
    emulate -L sh
    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
  else
    local -
    set -euo pipefail
  fi

  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR PLUGIN_DIR QEMU_SRC PLUGIN_SO SIMPOINT_SRC SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH
  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"
  PLUGIN_DIR="$ROOT/qemu_plugin"
  QEMU_SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
  PLUGIN_SO="$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  SIMPOINT_SRC="$ROOT/tools/simpoint"
  SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH="$SIMPOINT_SRC/bin/simpoint"

  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

  clean_qemu()   { step "cleaning qemu build ($QEMU_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$QEMU_BIN_DIR"; ok "qemu cleaned"; }
  clean_vsim()   { step "cleaning vsim build ($VSIM_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$VSIM_BIN_DIR"; ok "vsim cleaned"; }
  clean_plugin() {
    step "cleaning plugin build ($PLUGIN_DIR)"
    if [ -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
      make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" clean
    else
      rm -f "$PLUGIN_SO" "$PLUGIN_DIR"/*.o
    fi
    ok "plugin cleaned"
  }
  clean_simpoint() {
    step "cleaning simpoint build ($SIMPOINT_SRC)"
    if [ -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
      make -C "$SIMPOINT_SRC" clean
    else
      rm -f "$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
    fi
    ok "simpoint cleaned"
  }

  if [ "${1:-}" = "clean" ]; then
    case "${2:-all}" in
      qemu)     clean_qemu ;;
      vsim)     clean_vsim ;;
      plugin)   clean_plugin ;;
      simpoint) clean_simpoint ;;
      all)      clean_qemu; clean_vsim; clean_plugin; clean_simpoint ;;
      *)        err "usage: source $_SETUP_SRC clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]"; return 1 ;;
    esac
    return 0
  fi

  step "1/4 building qemu"
  "$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

  step "2/4 building vsim"
  "$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

  step "3/4 building hybrid plugin"
  if [ ! -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
    err "missing $PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile"
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h" ]; then
    err "missing $QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h - hybrid_qemu submodule not initialized"
    return 1
  fi
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_SRC"
  ok "plugin -> $PLUGIN_SO"

  step "4/4 building simpoint clustering binary"
  if [ ! -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
    err "missing $SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile - tools/simpoint submodule not initialized"
    return 1
  fi
  "$ROOT/build_simpoint.sh"
  ok "simpoint -> $SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"

  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

  step "env: exporting VSIM_QEMU / VSIM_PLUGIN / SIMPOINT_BIN for e2e scripts"
  export VSIM_QEMU="$QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"
  export VSIM_PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_SO"
  export SIMPOINT_BIN="$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
  ok "VSIM_QEMU=$VSIM_QEMU"
  ok "VSIM_PLUGIN=$VSIM_PLUGIN"
  ok "SIMPOINT_BIN=$SIMPOINT_BIN"

  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
  fi
}

_hybrid_setup_main "$@"
unset -f _hybrid_setup_main 2>/dev/null
unset _SETUP_SRC

  step "0/4 loading per-host config"
  if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_FILE" ]; then
    if [ ! -f "$CONFIG_TEMPLATE" ]; then
      err "missing $CONFIG_TEMPLATE; cannot create $CONFIG_FILE"
      return 1
    fi
    cp "$CONFIG_TEMPLATE" "$CONFIG_FILE"
    ok "created $CONFIG_FILE from template -- edit it if your toolchain lives elsewhere"
  fi
  # shellcheck source=/dev/null
  . "$CONFIG_FILE"
  if [ -z "${HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN:-}" ]; then
    err "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN not set; check $CONFIG_FILE"
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ! -d "$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN/bin" ]; then
    err "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN has no bin/ directory; edit $CONFIG_FILE"
    return 1
  fi
  export HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN
  ok "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN"

  step "1/4 building qemu"
toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Driver: build qemu + vsim + plugin + simpoint, then add build dirs to PATH.
# Source this script to affect your current shell's PATH:
#   source ./setup.sh                       # build all
#   source ./setup.sh clean [target]        # clean qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all (default: all)

if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${(%):-%x}"
elif [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
  _SETUP_SRC="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"
else
  _SETUP_SRC="$0"
fi

# Wrap the body in a function so strict-mode options stay local and don't
# leak into the caller's interactive shell (which would break zsh prompts
# under nounset).
_hybrid_setup_main() {
  if [ -n "${ZSH_VERSION:-}" ]; then
    emulate -L sh
    setopt err_exit no_unset pipe_fail
  else
    local -
    set -euo pipefail
  fi

  local ROOT QEMU_BIN_DIR VSIM_BIN_DIR PLUGIN_DIR QEMU_SRC PLUGIN_SO SIMPOINT_SRC SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH CONFIG_FILE CONFIG_TEMPLATE
  ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$_SETUP_SRC")" && pwd)"
  QEMU_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/build/qemu"
  VSIM_BIN_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim/build"
  PLUGIN_DIR="$ROOT/qemu_plugin"
  QEMU_SRC="$ROOT/hybrid_qemu"
  PLUGIN_SO="$PLUGIN_DIR/libhybrid_handoff.so"
  SIMPOINT_SRC="$ROOT/tools/simpoint"
  SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH="$SIMPOINT_SRC/bin/simpoint"
  CONFIG_FILE="$ROOT/config.env"
  CONFIG_TEMPLATE="$ROOT/config.env.example"

  step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m \033[1m%s\033[0m\n' "$*"; }
  ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
  err()  { printf '\033[1;31m[err]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

  clean_qemu()   { step "cleaning qemu build ($QEMU_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$QEMU_BIN_DIR"; ok "qemu cleaned"; }
  clean_vsim()   { step "cleaning vsim build ($VSIM_BIN_DIR)";   rm -rf "$VSIM_BIN_DIR"; ok "vsim cleaned"; }
  clean_plugin() {
    step "cleaning plugin build ($PLUGIN_DIR)"
    if [ -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
      make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" clean
    else
      rm -f "$PLUGIN_SO" "$PLUGIN_DIR"/*.o
    fi
    ok "plugin cleaned"
  }
  clean_simpoint() {
    step "cleaning simpoint build ($SIMPOINT_SRC)"
    if [ -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
      make -C "$SIMPOINT_SRC" clean
    else
      rm -f "$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
    fi
    ok "simpoint cleaned"
  }

  if [ "${1:-}" = "clean" ]; then
    case "${2:-all}" in
      qemu)     clean_qemu ;;
      vsim)     clean_vsim ;;
      plugin)   clean_plugin ;;
      simpoint) clean_simpoint ;;
      all)      clean_qemu; clean_vsim; clean_plugin; clean_simpoint ;;
      *)        err "usage: source $_SETUP_SRC clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]"; return 1 ;;
    esac
    return 0
  fi

  step "1/4 building qemu"
  "$ROOT/build_qemu.sh"
  ok "qemu -> $QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"

  step "2/4 building vsim"
  "$ROOT/build_vsim.sh"
  ok "vsim -> $VSIM_BIN_DIR/vsim"

  step "3/4 building hybrid plugin"
  if [ ! -f "$PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile" ]; then
    err "missing $PLUGIN_DIR/Makefile"
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ! -f "$QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h" ]; then
    err "missing $QEMU_SRC/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h - hybrid_qemu submodule not initialized"
    return 1
  fi
  make -C "$PLUGIN_DIR" QEMU_ROOT="$QEMU_SRC"
  ok "plugin -> $PLUGIN_SO"

  step "4/4 building simpoint clustering binary"
  if [ ! -f "$SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile" ]; then
    err "missing $SIMPOINT_SRC/Makefile - tools/simpoint submodule not initialized"
    return 1
  fi
  "$ROOT/build_simpoint.sh"
  ok "simpoint -> $SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"

  step "PATH: prepending build dirs"
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$QEMU_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$QEMU_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":$VSIM_BIN_DIR:"*) ;;
    *) export PATH="$VSIM_BIN_DIR:$PATH" ;;
  esac
  ok "PATH updated (this shell only)"

  step "env: exporting VSIM_QEMU / VSIM_PLUGIN / SIMPOINT_BIN for e2e scripts"
  export VSIM_QEMU="$QEMU_BIN_DIR/qemu-system-riscv64"
  export VSIM_PLUGIN="$PLUGIN_SO"
  export SIMPOINT_BIN="$SIMPOINT_BIN_PATH"
  ok "VSIM_QEMU=$VSIM_QEMU"
  ok "VSIM_PLUGIN=$VSIM_PLUGIN"
  ok "SIMPOINT_BIN=$SIMPOINT_BIN"

  if [ -n "${BASH_VERSION:-}" ] && [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-}" = "${0:-}" ]; then
    err "script was executed, not sourced - PATH change is local to this subshell"
    err "to update your shell, run: source $_SETUP_SRC"
  fi
}

_hybrid_setup_main "$@"
unset -f _hybrid_setup_main 2>/dev/null
unset _SETUP_SRC

+    err "HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN=$HYBRID_TOOLCHAIN has no bin/ directory; edit $CONFIG_FILE"

attachment.snippet:
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	* Always update docs/USER_GUIDE.md if something changed.
5	
6	## Repository Overview
7	
8	Andes hybrid RISC-V co-simulator: QEMU 9.x (functional, MHz-level) + vsim (SystemC + Verilator, cycle-accurate) share architectural state across a magic-CSR boundary. The system runs a three-phase round-trip: **QEMU1** (drain on CSR/PC trigger) -> **vsim** (resume + execute + drain) -> **QEMU2** (restore via gdbstub, continue).
9	
10	Two Git submodules:
11	- `hybrid_qemu/` - QEMU fork hosting the TCG plugin (branch: `hybrid`)
12	- `hybrid_vsim/` - vsim sources; `hybrid/` subdirs hold the plugin and test fixtures
13	- `tools/simpoint/` - SimPoint 3.2 (UCSD) clustering binary; pinned to upstream `17783e5`
14	
15	Shared ABI lives at `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (not inside either submodule).
16	
17	## Build Commands
18	
19	```sh
20	# Build everything (qemu -> vsim -> plugin -> simpoint)
21	source ./setup.sh
22	
23	# Build individual components
24	./build_qemu.sh                              # QEMU (configure + make -j64)
25	./build_vsim.sh                              # vsim (Docker/podman, runs ./build.sh inside container)
26	make -C qemu_plugin QEMU_ROOT=./hybrid_qemu  # TCG plugin (out-of-tree)
27	./build_simpoint.sh                          # SimPoint clustering binary (g++ on host)
28	
29	# Build RISC-V ELF test fixtures
30	make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test              # all fixtures
31	make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test rt_c_v_regs.elf  # single fixture
32	
33	# Clean
34	source ./setup.sh clean [qemu|vsim|plugin|simpoint|all]
35	```
36	
37	**Outputs:**
38	- `build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64`
39	- `hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium` (or other CPU variant)
40	- `qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so`
41	- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
42	
43	`build_vsim.sh` is Docker-based (podman). It builds an image from `hybrid_vsim/Dockerfile` (target `env`) then runs `./build.sh` inside it, mounting the repo at `/work` and `include/` as `/include:ro`.
44	
45	## Running Tests
46	
47	```sh
48	# All e2e tests in parallel (auto-detects job count from nproc + /dev/shm headroom)
49	bash run_e2e.sh
50	
51	# Run with specific options
52	JOBS=4 MODE=csr bash run_e2e.sh         # 4-way parallel, CSR-mode only
53	JOBS=1 bash run_e2e.sh                  # sequential (debug)
54	FILTER=rt_c_v bash run_e2e.sh           # regex filter on fixture name
55	LOG_DIR=/tmp/my-logs bash run_e2e.sh    # custom log directory
56	
57	# Unit tests (inside vsim build container)
58	# The container build runs: cmake --build build -j
59	# To run ctest directly after build:
60	#   podman run ... ./build.sh && podman exec ... ctest --test-dir build -L hybrid-e2e
61	```
62	
63	**Important:** on a high-core-count machine (128+ cores) `default_jobs()` computes `min(nproc, /dev/shm_free/256MB)` which can reach 128+. Running that many vsim Verilator processes simultaneously saturates CPU and causes timeouts. Use `JOBS=8` (or lower) as a practical cap: `JOBS=8 bash run_e2e.sh`.
64	
65	`run_e2e.sh` exits 0 only when all tests pass. Logs go to a `mktemp` directory under `/tmp`; path is printed at the start of each run. Each test's full output lands in `$LOG_DIR/$mode--$name.log`.
66	
67	## Architecture
68	
69	### Three-Phase Handoff
70	
71	```
72	QEMU1 --(state file)--> vsim --(state file + V-scratch mmap)--> QEMU2
73	```
74	
75	1. **QEMU plugin** (`qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c`) - TCG plugin. On CSR-write trigger (`csrwi 0x7C0, 0`) or PC-trigger, drains QEMU's architectural state into a binary `hybrid_state_v1` struct written to `--outfile`.
76	2. **vsim handoff controller** (`src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp`) - Detects the trigger inside vsim, pauses the hart via DMI, then calls `ResumeDriver` to restore state.
77	3. **ResumeDriver** (`src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`) - Writes GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-state into the vsim hart via RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands and Program Buffer.
78	4. **StateDrain** (`src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`) - On vsim exit trigger, reads back the full architectural state via DMI Abstract Commands.
79	5. **qemu_handback** (`src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp`) - Spawns QEMU2, connects via gdbstub, sends P-packet register writes for GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/V-regs, then `continue`s.
80	
81	### Trigger Modes
82	
83	- **CSR mode** (default): Pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (entry) / `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (exit) at retire time. CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on Andes ax45mp.
84	- **PC mode**: ELF symbols `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` resolved at startup; CSR insns are bypassed.
85	
86	Both modes coexist in the fixtures. `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc` selects at runtime.
87	
88	### Shared ABI (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`)
89	
90	`hybrid_state_v1` is the binary wire format exchanged between QEMU plugin, vsim, and the handback path. It holds: 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, etc.), PMP (64 pmpaddr + 16 pmpcfg), and the V extension block (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, 32 vreg[], vxsat, vxrm, vcsr). `HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64` supports VLEN up to 512 bits.
91	
92	Flags (`HYBRID_FLAG_RV64`, `HYBRID_FLAG_FP`, `HYBRID_FLAG_V`) gate which state blocks are live. The ABI struct is validated by `tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp`.
93	
94	### V-State Transport
95	
96	V register transport uses a fixed scratch region (`kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`) in the shared mmap. vsim's inline asm writes all 32 V regs there via `vse8.v`; `StateDrain` reads those bytes into `hybrid_state_v1::vreg[]`. `qemu_handback` ships them to QEMU2 via gdbstub P-packets by register number.
97	
98	V scalar CSR transport (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr):
99	- **Tier A** (Abstract Commands): vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, mstatus.VS
100	- **Tier B** (Program Buffer): vtype + vl via `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` (direct CSR write to vtype sets vill; vl is read-only; only `vsetvl` restores both atomically)
101	
102	Restore order in `ResumeDriver::resume()` when `HYBRID_FLAG_V` is set:
103	1. `mstatus.VS` (enables V in the hart)
104	2. `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in Program Buffer (stages `vl` in x5, `vtype` in x6 via abstract GPR writes first)
105	3. `vstart` (AFTER vsetvl: vsetvl resets vstart to 0)
106	4. `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`
107	5. GPR loop (overwrites the x5/x6 scratch - safe now)
108	
109	### Test Fixture Tiers
110	
111	| Tier | Harness | Memory model | Examples |
112	|------|---------|-------------|---------|
113	| 1 | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` | QEMU-private RAM | handoff_roundtrip, rt_all_gprs, rt_csr_survival |
114	| 2 | `rt_shared_mem.sh` | shared `/dev/shm` mmap | rt_c_hello, rt_c_matmul, rt_c_v_regs, rt_c_v_csrs |
115	
116	Tier-2 is required for C-level fixtures because the C stack and heap must survive the vsim -> QEMU2 boundary. The shared mmap is created with `mktemp -p /dev/shm` + `truncate`, then QEMU is launched with `-object memory-backend-file`.
117	
118	Fixture source lives in `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/`. C-level fixtures link `runtime/{crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c}` plus the Andes toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting-backed libgloss), using `rt_c.ld`. V-using fixtures require `-march=rv64gcv`. **Phase-2 rule:** the span between `enter_vsim()` and `exit_vsim()` runs on vsim and MUST NOT issue any syscall (no `printf`, `fopen`, semihosting ebreak, etc.) -- vsim does not implement semihosting, so any libgloss-routed call inside the ROI traps. Phase 1 and phase 3 print via standard newlib stdio (both QEMUs are launched with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`).
119	
120	### vsim CMake Structure
121	
122	vsim lives entirely in `hybrid_vsim/`. Key CMake options:
123	- `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID` (default OFF) - enables e2e CTest registration
124	- `VSIM_CONFIG_FILE` - CPU YAML (default `config.yaml`)
125	- `VSIM_NO_VERILATOR` - skip Verilator build (assume prebuilt)
126	
127	The hybrid orchestration layer (`src/hybrid/*.hpp`) is header-only and depends only on C++20 standard library + the shared ABI header. SystemC/Verilator are not required for hybrid unit tests.
128

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T test.ld -nostdlib -static

# All fixtures share test.ld (load base 0x00100000, andes_ae350 DRAM).
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump demo

all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Note: handoff.elf, handoff_exit.elf, spin.elf are legacy (referenced only
# by EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL handoff_smoke.cpp). They still build for completeness
# but are not in active CTest. All targets now share test.ld at 0x00100000.

# Switching-mode demo runner: builds the fixtures if needed, then walks
# T1..T7 from $(DEMO_DIR), printing a banner per mode and a final
# pass/fail tally. Honors FILTER=<regex> to subset the run.
demo: all
	@bash demo.sh

spin.elf: spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

# CSR-less spin for F5 plugin-icount e2e. Linked at 0x00100000 (test.ld)
# so andes_ae350's MROM at 0x80000000 stays clear when loaded via -bios.
icount_spin.elf: icount_spin.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c icount_spin.S -o icount_spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) icount_spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (newlib pulled). crt0.S is
# passed FIRST so _start lands at the load address (0x00100000) -- ld
# preserves input order within the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# RVV-enabled fixtures: same runtime + v_regfile_xfer.c, different
# -march. Listed separately so changes to RT_C_CFLAGS don't accidentally
# drop V.
$(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) test.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_V_CFLAGS) -T test.ld \
	  $(RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS) $< $(RT_C_LIBS) -o $@

# Probe fixtures: pure asm, -march=rv64gcv (need V CSR names), no C runtime.
PROBE_V_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
$(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S test.ld
	$(CC) $(PROBE_V_CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -machine andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

# F-T2.4: mcycle/minstret must strictly increase from QEMU1's drain to
# vsim's drain. Offsets locked by state_abi_check.h (mcycle@624,
# minstret@632). Opt-in via env var. Runs before the RC gate so the
# verdict is independent of any downstream-phase failure.
if [ "${CHECK_MCYCLE_MONOTONIC:-0}" = "1" ] && [ -s "$STATE_VSIM" ]; then
  read_u64_le() { od -A n -j "$1" -N 8 -t u8 "$2" | tr -d ' \n'; }
  Q1_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_QEMU")
  Q1_MINST=$(read_u64_le 632 "$STATE_QEMU")
  V_MCYCLE=$(read_u64_le 624 "$STATE_VSIM")
  V_MINST=$(read_u64_le 632 "$STATE_VSIM")
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] qemu1: mcycle=$Q1_MCYCLE minstret=$Q1_MINST"
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] vsim:  mcycle=$V_MCYCLE minstret=$V_MINST"
  if [ "$V_MCYCLE" -le "$Q1_MCYCLE" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: mcycle not strictly increasing across vsim"
    echo "  qemu1=$Q1_MCYCLE vsim=$V_MCYCLE"
    exit 1
  fi
  if [ "$V_MINST" -le "$Q1_MINST" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: minstret not strictly increasing across vsim"
    echo "  qemu1=$Q1_MINST vsim=$V_MINST"
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "[$FIXTURE mcycle/minstret] PASS: strictly increasing"
fi

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"
